title: 21s Bicep Curl
howTo: Grab a straight or curved bar evenly and stand tall. Bring your shoulders back, squeeze your glutes, and take a breath. Curl the weight halfway (to the point where your wrists align with your elbows) for seven repetitions. Immediately start curling the bar from the halfway point to the top (where your wrists are a couple of inches higher than your elbows) for another seven repetitions. Finish by doing seven complete repetitions from full arm extension to the top position, breathing steadily as you do.

title: Ab Scissors
howTo: Sit on an exercise mat, lean back, and place your hands on the floor and to your sides for balance. Engage your abs and raise your feet several inches off the floor while keeping your knees extended. Take a breath and cross your legs by bringing the right one over the left. Immediately uncross them and move the left leg over the right. Keep alternating until you get tired, breathing steadily as you do reps.

title: Ab Wheel
howTo: Grab an ab wheel and get down on your knees. Lean forward and place the ab wheel on the floor while holding it firmly with both hands. Engage your abs and take a breath. Roll the ab wheel forward as you extend your body. Move forward as much as your ab strength allows and roll the wheel back to the starting position. Exhale.

title: Arnold Press (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of light dumbbells and sit on a gym bench with an upright back support. Lift the dumbbells and rest them on your thighs. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Thrust the dumbbells and bring them to your sides. Take a breath and press the dumbbells to an overhead position as you breathe out. Lower the dumbbells slowly as you rotate your wrists in, breathing in on the way down. You should end up with the dumbbells in front of your chin and palms facing your body. Press again, rotating your wrists to complete the repetition with your palms facing forward.

title: Around The World
howTo: Lie on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Position the dumbbells over your upper thighs with your arms straight and palms facing down. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and breathe in. Slowly move the dumbbells from your upper thighs to behind your head in an arc motion, keeping your arms straight. Breathe out. Immediately move the weights from behind your head to the starting position in an arc as you breathe in. While doing so, twist your wrists to have your palms facing down at the starting position.

title: Assisted Pistol Squats
howTo: Stand next to a squat rack and grab it with one hand for balance. Lift one foot off the floor and raise the leg a few inches forward. Keep your opposite foot firmly planted on the floor. Engage your abs, bring your shoulders back, and take a breath. Squat by bending your knee and moving the opposite leg forward, keeping the foot off the floor. Descend as much as possible and push through the heel to lift yourself as you exhale. Once finished training one leg, support yourself on the opposite foot and perform the same number of reps.

title: Back Extension (Hyperextension)
howTo: Set yourself up on a back extension machine. Have your hips and thighs against the pad and your ankles locked on the foot brace. Engage your abs, cross your arms in front of your chest, and take a breath. Lower yourself by bending at the hips and move down until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings and glutes. Engage your buttocks and lower back to raise your torso to the top position, pausing for a moment and exhaling.

title: Back Extension (Machine)
howTo: Sit on a back extension machine with your buttocks against a pad. Place the foot pad just over your feet. The top pad should be against your upper back. Adjust the load on the machine and cross your arms in front of your chest. Take a breath, engage your abs, and push the top pad backward by engaging your lower back muscles. Breathe out. Lean forward to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Back Extension (Weighted Hyperextension)
howTo: Set yourself up inside a back extension bench. Have your heels secured against a pad and your hips against another pad. Bend forward and grab a weight plate off the floor or have a training partner hand it to you. Extend your body and take a breath. Bend your body at the hips while keeping your shoulders back and spine neutral. Move down as much as your muscle flexibility allows. Extend your body by engaging your posterior chain and exhale near the top.

title: Ball Slams
howTo: Stand tall with your feet in a comfortable stance and your toes pointing slightly out. Hold a medicine ball in both hands, keeping it at stomach level. Engage your midsection, bend your knees slightly, and thrust the medicine ball overhead. Your arms should be straight, and the ball should be directly overhead for balance. Inhale and use your upper body muscles to accelerate the ball down and slam it as hard as you can just in front of your body. Breathe out. Bend your knees enough to pick up the ball while keeping your spine neutral. Breathe in and repeat the sequence.

title: Band Pullaparts
howTo: Grab a looped or open-ended resistance band with handles on both ends. The band should offer moderate resistance. Extend your arms in front of your body and have your palms neutral (facing one another). Take a breath and pull the band apart, bringing your arms to the sides while keeping them straight. Exhale. Bring your arms together as you breathe in.

title: Battle Ropes
howTo: Grab the pair of battle ropes. Bend your knees and lean forward slightly while keeping your spine neutral. Take a breath and move one arm up to shoulder level while bringing the opposite one down to your knees. Immediately move both arms in the opposite direction, changing their position. Alternate your arms at a steady tempo, breathing regularly as you do. Go for as long as possible until you feel a deep burn in your upper back and shoulders.

title: Behind the Back Bicep Wrist Curl (Barbell)
howTo: Place a loaded barbell or EZ bar on the floor and stand in front of it. With your arms to your sides and palms facing back, squat, and grab the bar evenly. Keep your back straight. Push through your heels and squat up, lifting the bar behind your body. Take a breath and flex your wrists to curl the barbell without moving your elbows or arms. Breathe out at the top. Extend your wrists as you breathe in.

title: Behind the Back Curl (Cable)
howTo: Set the pulleys at around mid-thigh height, attach handles, and select the load. Grab the handles one by one and stand so the pulleys are behind you. Take a couple of steps forward to lift the weights off their stacks and allow the weight to pull your elbows behind your torso. With your chest out, lean your torso slightly forward and stagger your stance (one foot forward, the other one back). Breathe in, engage your abs, and curl until your wrists are at elbow level. Squeeze your biceps and exhale. Slowly extend your arms, allowing the handles to travel behind your body, and breathe in. Repeat.

title: Belt Squat (Machine)
howTo: Load the machine appropriately and put on the weight belt. Step on the platform and hook the belt’s chain to the machine’s lever via a carabiner. Stand tall and grab the handles or horizontal bar in front. Position your feet at a comfortable width for squats with your toes pointing slightly out. Bring your chest out, engage your abs, and unrack the weight. You can hold onto the bar or handles in front of you for balance. Breathe in and descend until your thighs are roughly parallel to the bar. Pause briefly and push through your heels to extend your knees, exhaling at the top. Breathe in and repeat.

title: Bench Dip
howTo: Face away from a sturdy gym bench, plyo box, or chair. Lean back and place your hands flat on the object's edge. Straighten your legs and support your lower body on your heels. Your torso should be upright. Engage your abs, take a breath, and descend by bending your elbows. Move down until your elbows form a 90-degree angle and pause for a moment. Extend your arms and exhale near the top.

title: Bench Press (Barbell)
howTo: Lie on the bench. Extend your arms and grab the bar evenly, having your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Bring your shoulder blades back and dig them into the bench. Arch your lower back and plant your feet flat on the floor. Take a breath, unrack the bar, and bring it over your chest. Inhale again and lower the barbell to your lower chest, tapping it slightly. Hold for a moment and press the bar until your elbows are straight. Exhale.

title: Bench Press (Cable)
howTo: Place a flat gym bench in the middle of a double cable machine. Adjust the load on both weight stacks, position the pulleys low, and attach handles. Grab both handles and sit on the bench while keeping your arms close to your body. Lie back carefully and bring your arms to your sides without flaring your elbows. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Push both handles up and in, tapping your knuckles at the top as you exhale. Lower the weights carefully until your elbows are at torso level, and breathe in. Once finished, bring your arms in, get up slowly, and release both handles.

title: Bench Press (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells, sit on a flat gym bench, and place both weights on top of your thighs. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, take a breath, and flex your arms. Kick both dumbbells up with your thighs as you lie back. With your arms straight and dumbbells over your chest, dig your shoulder blades into the bench and place your feet flat on the floor. Take another breath and lower both dumbbells to your sides. Keep your elbows somewhat tucked. Lower the weights until your elbows are at torso level. Press both dumbbells to the starting position, bringing them together as you exhale.

title: Bench Press (Smith Machine)
howTo: Place a flat gym bench in the middle of a Smith machine. Adjust the bar to a height where you can grab and unrack it from a lying position. Lie on the bench and position your lower chest directly underneath the bar. Grab the bar with an even overhand grip. Bring your shoulders back and dig the balls of your feet into the floor. Take a breath and unrack the bar. Lower the bar until it taps your chest and press it to the top as you exhale.

title: Bench Press - Close Grip (Barbell)
howTo: Set the barbell at a height where you can reach it from a lying position. Lie on the bench and position your head underneath the bar. Reach up and grab the bar with an even, overhand grip. Your hands should be shoulder-width apart. Bring your shoulder blades back and draw your feet back and toward the bench, digging the balls of your feet into the floor. Engage your body, unrack the bar, and bring it over your chest. Breathe in and lower the barbell to your lower chest. Press the bar to the top position and exhale. Once finished, bring the barbell over the rack and rest it gently.

title: Bench Press - Wide Grip (Barbell)
howTo: Set the barbell at a height where you can grab it from a lying position. Lie on the bench and position yourself so that the bar is directly over your forehead. Reach up and grab the bar with an overhand grip. Start with your hands shoulder-width apart, and gradually increase your grip width. Retract your shoulder blades and dig them into the bench. Bring your feet back and in toward the bench, digging the balls of your feet into the floor. Engage your upper body, unrack the barbell, and bring it over your chest. Take a breath and lower the barbell to the bottom (lower chest, nipple line). Tap your torso and push the bar back to the top, squeezing your triceps, shoulders, and pectorals. Exhale near the top. Once finished, bring the bar back over the safety pins and rack it.

title: Bent Over Row (Band)
howTo: Take an open-ended resistance band with handles on both sides. Step over the middle of the band with both feet. Grab both handles and lean forward while keeping your spine neutral. The band should offer some tension even with your arms fully extended. You can increase or decrease the tension by having your feet closer or wider apart. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Pull both ends of the band up and in until your elbows are at torso level. Exhale at the top. Extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Bent Over Row (Barbell)
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell with your feet in a comfortable stance and toes pointing slightly out. Lean forward by hinging at the hip and keep your spine in a neutral position. Grab the barbell with an even overhand grip. Engage your abs and lift the bar several inches off the floor. With your shoulders back and midsection tight, take a breath and row the barbell. Lift the bar until it taps your stomach and hold the position for a moment as you exhale. Lower the bar slowly.

title: Bent Over Row (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and lean forward while keeping your back straight. Your torso should be as parallel to the floor as possible. Have your arms straight and hanging down with your wrists neutral (facing one another). Take a breath and row both dumbbells until your elbows are at torso level. Exhale at the top. Lower the weights, breathing in on the way down.

title: Bicep Curl (Barbell)
howTo: Pick the bar up and hold it with a grip slightly wider than your hips. Your arms should be straight, with your palms facing forward. Straighten your back, bring your chest out, take a breath, and curl the barbell, exhaling at the top. Don’t use body swinging and momentum to lift the bar; only your bicep strength. Lower the bar slowly as you breathe in, extending your arms and stretching your biceps. Repeat.

title: Bicep Curl (Cable)
howTo: Attach a straight bar to a low cable pulley. Set the load on the weight stack at the appropriate amount. Lean forward and grab the straight bar evenly with your palms facing up. Stand up and step back (if necessary) to lift the weight from its stack. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and have your elbows to your sides. Take a breath and curl the weight, keeping your elbows steady. Lift the weight until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows and exhale. Lower the weight slowly, breathing in on the way down.

title: Bicep Curl (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall with your feet in a comfortable stance and shoulders retracted. Have your wrists pointing forward and your arms extended. Take a breath and curl both dumbbells simultaneously. Lift the weights until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows and exhale. Extend your arms slowly and breathe in on the way down.

title: Bicep Curl (Machine)
howTo: Select the correct load on the machine and adjust your seat’s height. Sit down and grab the handles by your sides. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Bend one arm to lift the handle. Curl until your hand is slightly higher than your elbow, and breathe out. Extend your arm slowly as you breathe in. Bend your opposite arm in the same way. Keep alternating until you finish the set.

title: Bicep Curl (Suspension)
howTo: Grab the pair of handles attached to a suspension training kit. Walk your feet forward to lean your body back to some degree. Extend your body, squeeze your glutes, and bring your shoulders back. Take a breath and bend your arms to lift your body. Bend your arms until your elbows are at slightly more than a 90-degree angle, and breathe out. Extend your arms slowly, lowering your body to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Bicycle Crunch
howTo: Lie on the floor, lift your legs off the floor, and bend your knees at a 90-degree angle. Place your fingertips behind your head or neck. Take a breath, engage your midsection, and crunch, bringing your right elbow to your left knee. Exhale. Extend your body as you breathe in. Crunch again, taking your left elbow to your right knee. Exhale again. Keep alternating until you complete the set.

title: Bicycle Crunch Raised Legs
howTo: Lie on the floor, lift your legs off the ground, and extend them in the air. Place your fingertips behind your head or neck. Take a breath, engage your midsection, and crunch your upper body. Twist your upper body, bringing your right elbow to your left leg. Simultaneously bend your left knee and get it closer to the opposite elbow. Hold for a moment as you breathe out. Extend your body and breathe in. Take your left elbow to your right knee. Keep alternating until you complete the set.

title: Bird Dog
howTo: Get down on all fours. Have your shoulders, elbows, and wrists aligned and your knees directly under your hips. Keep your hands flat on the floor. Engage your abs and take a breath. Extend your right leg back and up, aligning it with your torso. At the same time, extend your left arm and raise it forward. Exhale. Bring your arm and leg to the starting position as you breathe in. Extend your opposite limbs. Keep alternating between opposite limbs until you complete the set.

title: Box Jump
howTo: Stand in front of a plyometric box or a flat gym bench. Have your feet close and your spine neutral. Take a breath and descend into a quarter squat as you bring your arms back. Immediately jump by pushing through your heels and swinging your arms forward. Exhale as you land on the bench or box. Step off and breathe in.

title: Box Squat (Barbell)
howTo: Set the barbell at the appropriate height and position a plyometric box behind you. Unrack the bar as you would for standard squats and take a couple of steps back. The box should be a few inches away from your heels. Take a breath and descend into a squat. Move down until your buttocks touch the box. Hold the position for a moment and push through your heels to squat up. Exhale.

title: Bulgarian Split Squat
howTo: Face away from a flat gym bench. Grab a pair of dumbbells and extend one leg back, placing the foot on the bench. Bring your shoulders back, engage your midsection, and take a breath. Squat by bending your front leg. Descend until your front thigh is almost parallel to the floor. Hold the bottom position for a moment and push through your heel to extend your leg as you exhale. Do the desired number of reps, switch legs, and match the rep count for your other leg.

title: Burpee
howTo: Stand tall with your feet in a comfortable stance, arms to your sides, and gaze directed forward. Take a breath, lean forward and bend your knees to place your hands flat on the floor, just in front of your feet. Kick your legs back to extend your body into a push-up position. Your shoulders, hips, knees, and ankles should be aligned. Inhale and perform a push-up, descending until your face is an inch or two from the floor (optional). Exhale as you extend your arms. Kick your legs forward to bring your feet just behind your hands as you breathe in. Extend your body and perform a vertical jump as you raise your arms above your head. Exhale.

title: Burpee Over the Bar
howTo: Place a barbell on the ground and stand perpendicular to it. Get down into a high plank position. With your body straight and shoulders, elbows, and wrists aligned, take a breath and do a push-up. Push yourself back to the high plank position as you exhale. Kick your feet forward and stand up. Jump laterally over the barbell as you breathe out again. Get down to a high plank position again, breathe in, and perform another push-up. Stand up and jump laterally over the barbell again, repeating the sequence. Keep jumping over the bar until you're finished.

title: Butterfly (Pec Deck)
howTo: Select the load on the pec deck machine. Adjust the seat height so you can grab the handles and have the pads in front of your chest. Sit down, grab the handles, and bring your shoulders back. Engage your abs and take a breath. Flex your chest muscles to bring the handles from your sides to in front of your torso as you breathe out. Bring your arms to your sides as you breathe in.

title: Cable Core Palloff Press
howTo: Select the load on the cable machine. Place the pulley at chest height and attach a handle. Grab the handle with both hands and stand sideways. Bring your shoulders back, squeeze your glutes, and engage your midsection. Take a breath and press the handle forward, fully extending your arms and exhaling. Pause for a moment and bring the handle to your body as you breathe in.

title: Cable Crunch
howTo: Grab a rope attachment with both hands and have your thumbs facing up. Kneel, lean forward, and position the rope behind your head. Keep your hands close to your neck. Once in position, engage your midsection. Initiate the crunch by taking a breath and flexing your abs. Focus on crunching your torso instead of simply moving at the hips. Hold for a moment as you exhale. Slowly raise your torso to bring yourself to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Cable Fly Crossovers
howTo: Adjust the load on the pair of weight stacks inside the cable crossover machine. Set the pulleys in the highest position and attach handles on both. Grab the handles one at a time and position yourself in the middle between the two pillars. Bring your arms to your sides and take a half step forward to lift the weights from their stacks. Engage your abs, bring your shoulders back, and inhale. Bring your arms in and down, meeting your knuckles in front of your hips. Exhale. Hold for a moment and bring your arms to your sides as you breathe in.

title: Cable Pull Through
howTo: Select the appropriate load on a single or double cable machine station. Set the pulley in the lowest position and attach a handle or rope. Face away from the cable machine, lean forward, grab the attachment, and hold it between your legs. Take a step or two forward to lift the weight from its stack. Engage your abs and take a breath. Hinge at the hips, leaning your torso forward while keeping your back neutral. Lean forward until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor and extend your body by driving your hips forward. Exhale as you stand tall.

title: Cable Twist (Down to up)
howTo: Select the appropriate load on the cable machine. Place the pulley in the lowest position and attach a handle to it. Grab the handle with both hands, keeping your wrists neutral (facing one another). Take a step away from the cable machine and stand sideways with your feet wide enough to create a stable base. With the handle at your waist and arms straight, engage your abs and take a breath. Pull the cable diagonally and upward across your body, twisting your torso and exhaling. Hold for a second and rotate your torso to the starting position as you breathe in. Once finished training one side, turn your body 180 degrees and do the same number of reps for the other side.

title: Cable Twist (Up to down)
howTo: Select the appropriate load on the cable machine. Place the pulley in the highest position and attach a handle to it. Grab the handle with both hands, keeping your wrists neutral (facing one another). Take a step away from the cable machine and stand sideways. With the handle at shoulder level and arms straight, engage your abs and breathe in. Pull the cable diagonally across your body, twisting your torso and exhaling. Rotate your body to the starting position as you breathe in. Once finished, turn your body 180 degrees and do the same number of reps for the other side.

title: Calf Extension (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load on the calf extension machine. Sit down and grab the handles by your sides. Place the balls of your feet on the footplate. Your legs should be straight. Take a breath and flex your ankles to push the plate away as you exhale. Hold for a moment and allow your ankles to bend as you breathe in.

title: Calf Press (Machine)
howTo: Load a leg press machine and sit down. Raise your legs and place the balls of your feet at the edge of the footplate. Bring your shoulders back and grab the handles by your sides. Extend your knees to unrack the weight. Flex your ankles to press the platform away from yourself and hold for a moment as you breathe out. Allow your ankles to bend and stretch your calves as you breathe in. Once finished, turn the handles you’re holding to secure the weight.

title: Chest Dip
howTo: Grip a parallel bar slightly outside shoulder-width level. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Raise yourself and stand over the dip bar with your elbows fully extended. Tilt your body forward. Take a breath and dip by bending at the elbows. Maintain your body’s position as you move down. Lower yourself until you feel a good pectoral stretch (generally to the point where your elbows are at a 90-degree angle). Push yourself back to the top, exhaling at the top.

title: Chest Dip (Assisted)
howTo: Select the correct weight on the chest dip machine. Grab the pair of handles and place your knees over the pads. Extend your arms, bring your shoulders back, and squeeze your abs. Take a breath and lower yourself by bending your elbows. Descend until your elbows are at roughly 90 degrees, and push yourself back to the top as you exhale.

title: Chest Dip (Weighted)
howTo: Attach a weight plate to yourself via a special belt. Step on the platform and grab the parallel bars while keeping your arms straight and close to your body. Bring your shoulders back and down, engage your abs, and squeeze your glutes. Take your feet off the platform to suspend yourself in the air. Lean forward slightly. Take a breath and lower yourself by bending your elbows. Keep your shoulder blades retracted and your body steady. Descend until your elbows form a 90-degree angle and hold for a moment. Press yourself back to the top, extending your arms fully and exhaling. Take another breath and repeat.

title: Chest Fly (Band)
howTo: Take an open-ended resistance band with handles on both ends. Anchor the band on something sturdy (e.g., a squat rack). Turn away from the anchor point and grab both handles, keeping them close to your body. Take a couple of steps forward to lengthen the band and bring your arms to your sides without flaring your elbows. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Move your arms forward and tap your knuckles in front of your chest while keeping your elbows slightly bent. Breathe out. Bring your arms to the sides as you breathe in.

title: Chest Fly (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of light dumbbells and sit on the edge of a flat gym bench. Place the weights on your thighs and lie back, keeping the dumbbells close to your body. Extend your arms and position the dumbbells over your chest with your palms facing one another. Plant your feet on the floor and bring your shoulders back. Take a breath. Lower your arms to your sides until they align with your torso. Bring your arms in, tapping the dumbbells over your chest as you exhale.

title: Chest Fly (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load and adjust the seat height. The machine’s handles should be at chest level when you sit. Sit down, bring your shoulders back, and reach to the sides to grab the handles. Engage your abs and take a breath. Squeeze your chest and bring your arms in, tapping your knuckles lightly in front of your chest as you breathe out. Bring your arms to your sides, feeling your chest muscles stretch. Breathe in.

title: Chest Fly (Suspension)
howTo: Face away from the anchor point of your suspension kit and grab both handles. Straighten your arms, raise them in front of your body, and engage your abs. Walk your feet back enough to lean your body forward. Support yourself with your arms. Take a breath and slowly bring your arms to your sides, lowering yourself and stretching your pectorals. Extend your arms to your sides as much as your strength allows, hold for a moment, and bring them in, raising yourself in the process. Tap your knuckles at the top position and exhale.

title: Chest Press (Band)
howTo: Take an open-ended resistance band with handle attachments. Anchor the band on something sturdy at chest height. A squat rack can work. Face away from the anchor point and grab both handles, raising your arms high and keeping your elbows tucked. Take a couple of steps forward to stretch the band and create some tension. Bring your shoulders back, stagger your stance, engage your midsection, and take a breath. Press the band forward and extend your arms fully as you exhale. Bend your arms, bringing your knuckles to the sides of your chest, as you breathe in.

title: Chest Press (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load on the machine. Adjust the seat height. The handles should be slightly below your lower chest when you sit down. Sit down, grab the handles, bring your shoulders back, and engage your abs. Have your feet flat on the floor. Take a breath and press until your arms extend fully. Exhale. Bend your arms slowly as you breathe in.

title: Chest Supported Incline Row (Dumbbell)
howTo: Set an adjustable gym bench at an incline of 30 to 45 degrees. Grab a pair of dumbbells and position yourself on the bench with your stomach and chest on the back support. Have your arms extended and hanging straight down. Bring your shoulders back, squeeze your glutes, and engage your abs. Rotate your wrists out so your knuckles face forward. Take a breath and row the dumbbells as high as possible in one fluid motion. Exhale at the top. Lower the weights slowly, breathing in on the way down.

title: Chest Supported Reverse Fly (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of light dumbbells and position yourself face down on an incline gym bench. Keep your body straight and support yourself on the balls of your feet. Your arms should be straight and vertical, with your palms facing one another. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Lift the dumbbells to your sides and back, stopping when you feel tension in the posterior of your shoulders. Exhale. Hold for a moment and bring your arms down slowly as you breathe in.

title: Chest Supported Y Raise (Dumbbell)
howTo: Set a gym bench at a 45-degree incline. Grab a pair of light dumbbells and sit down with your chest and stomach against the back support and arms hanging straight down. Take a deep breath and lift the dumbbells forward and up in one fluid motion, exhaling at the top. Lower the weights to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Chin Up
howTo: Reach up and grab a pull-up bar with a double underhand grip (palms facing back). Your hands should be shoulder-width apart or slightly closer. Retract your shoulders, engage your abs, squeeze your glutes, and cross your lower legs, lifting your feet off the floor. Take a breath and pull yourself up in one fluid motion. Aim to have your chin over the bar at the top. Exhale. Lower yourself slowly and breathe in. Don’t rest your feet on the floor.

title: Chin Up (Assisted)
howTo: Select the appropriate load on the machine. Reach up and grab the bar with a double underhand grip (palms facing back). Have your hands shoulder-width apart. Place your knees on top of the machine pad. Bring your shoulders back, squeeze your glutes, and engage your abs. Take a breath and pull yourself up in one fluid motion, exhaling at the top. Lower yourself slowly, extending your arms fully. Breathe in.

title: Chin Up (Weighted)
howTo: Attach a weight plate to yourself with a special weight belt. Reach up and grab a pull-up bar with a double underhand grip (palms facing back). Retract your shoulders, engage your abs, squeeze your glutes, and cross your lower legs, lifting your feet off the floor. Take a breath and pull yourself up in one fluid motion as you breathe out. Lower yourself slowly, extending your arms fully, and breathe in. Don’t rest your feet on the floor.

title: Clamshell
howTo: Lie on one side with your legs stacked, knees bent, bottom shoulder at 90 degrees, and the forearm flat on the floor for balance. You can wrap a mini looped resistance band over your thighs for additional resistance. Maintain a neutral spine and breathe in. Raise your top knee while keeping your feet together. The movement should resemble a clamshell opening. Lift your knee as much as possible in one fluid motion and exhale. Lower your knee to the bottom as you breathe in. Once finished, flip to your other side and do the same number of reps.

title: Clap Push Ups
howTo: Get down on all fours and extend your body into a push-up position. Your body should be straight, and your shoulders, elbows, and wrists should be aligned. Bring your shoulders back and squeeze your buttocks. Take a breath and lower yourself by bending your elbows. Descend until your face is an inch or two from the floor and hold for a moment. Push yourself as explosively as possible, lifting your hands off the floor. Breathe out. Immediately bring your hands together and clap before planting them on the floor. Breathe in as you lower yourself.

title: Clean
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell with your shins a couple of inches away. Your feet should be hip-width apart with toes pointed slightly out. Bend down and grab the barbell with an even, overhand grip. Have your knees against the crease of your elbows. Bring your chest out to put your back in a neutral position. Your hips should be slightly higher than your knees. Initiate the first pull by deadlifting the barbell off the floor as you press your heels into the ground. Make the initial pull powerful to generate momentum for the next part of the lift. Once the barbell travels above your knees, keep driving your hips forward as you begin pulling the weight in a straight vertical line. The entire sequence should happen in one smooth motion. As the barbell travels up, squat, catch it, and have it land over your shoulders and upper chest. Maintain a firm grip on the barbell, and keep your elbows pointing forward. As the barbell lands in a front rack position, dip a few extra inches until your thighs are parallel to the floor and squat to the top, straightening your knees fully and exhaling. Lower the barbell slowly and hinge at the hip to maintain a neutral spine. Breathe in as the bar moves down or before initiating the next rep.

title: Clean and Jerk
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell with your shins a couple of inches away. Your feet should be hip-width apart, with toes pointed slightly out. Bend down and grab the barbell with an even, overhand grip. Have your knees against the crease of your elbows. Bring your chest out to put your back in a neutral position. Your hips should be slightly higher than your knees. Initiate the first pull by deadlifting the barbell off the floor as you press your heels into the ground. Make the initial pull powerful to generate momentum for the next part of the lift. Once the barbell travels above your knees, keep driving your hips forward as you begin pulling the weight in a straight vertical line. The entire sequence should happen in one smooth motion. As the barbell travels up, squat, catch it, and have it land over your shoulders and upper chest. Maintain a firm grip on the barbell, and keep your elbows pointing forward. As the barbell lands in a front rack position, dip a few extra inches until your thighs are parallel to the floor and squat to the top, straightening your knees fully and breathing out. Take another breath, dip slightly, and jerk the bar, bringing it over your head, extending your elbows and knees. Lower the barbell to your shoulders and breathe out. Bring the bar to the floor while keeping your back neutral. Breathe in on the way down or before initiating the next rep.

title: Clean and Press
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell with your shins a couple of inches away. Your feet should be hip-width apart, with toes pointed slightly out. Lean forward and grab the barbell with an even, overhand grip. Have your knees against the crease of your elbows. Bring your chest out to put your back in a neutral position. Your hips should be slightly higher than your knees. Initiate the first pull by deadlifting the barbell off the floor as you press your heels into the ground. Make the initial pull powerful to generate momentum for the next part of the lift. Once the barbell travels above your knees, keep driving your hips forward as you begin pulling the weight in a straight vertical line. The entire sequence should happen in one smooth motion. As the barbell travels up, squat, catch it, and have it land over your shoulders and upper chest. Maintain a firm grip on the barbell and keep your elbows pointing forward. As the barbell lands in a front rack position, dip a few extra inches until your thighs are parallel to the floor and squat to the top, straightening your knees fully and exhaling. Take another breath and press the bar straight up, bringing your head back to avoid bumping your nose or chin. Exhale again. Lower the bar to your shoulders before returning it to the starting position.

title: Clean Pull
howTo: Stand in front of a barbell. Your feet should be hip-width apart and the barbell should be over the mid-foot. Lean forward and grab the barbell with an even overhand grip. Bring your shoulders back to straighten your back, lower yourself to the bar, and pull the slack off the barbell. Take a breath and initiate the pull by digging your heels into the floor. Extend your hips and knees while maintaining a neutral back position. As the barbell travels up, extend your knees and hips. Once the barbell goes over your knees, initiate the second stage by forcefully pressing your heels into the floor as if you were trying to do a vertical jump. As the barbell continues moving up, drive your hips forward, fully extending your knees and hips. Immediately shrug your shoulders and pull upward until the bar goes slightly above your waist. Exhale forcefully. Lower the barbell slowly by first bringing your glutes back. Keep your chest out and back straight. Breathe in on the way down or before initiating the next rep.

title: Concentration Curl
howTo: Take a light dumbbell and sit on a flat gym bench. Spread your legs wide and place the dumbbell between your legs. Reach down and grab the weight with your right hand. Lift the weight off the floor and place the right tricep (upper arm) against your inner leg. Keep your chest out and body stable, take a breath, and straighten your arm. Curl the dumbbell until your wrist is higher than your elbow. Keep your upper arm in the same position against your inner leg. Squeeze your muscle at the top as you breathe out. Lower the dumbbell slowly and extend your elbow entirely as you breathe in. Once finished, do the same number of reps for your other arm.

title: Cross Body Hammer Curl
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Have your feet in a normal stance, arms to your sides, and palms facing your body. Bring your shoulders back, squeeze your buttocks, and take a breath. Curl one dumbbell across your body and in front of your chest as you breathe out. Extend your arm as you breathe in. Curl the other dumbbell and alternate until you finish the set.

title: Crunch
howTo: Lie on the floor, bend your knees, and place your feet flat on the floor. Bring your arms up and place your fingertips behind your head. Engage your abs and take a breath. Crunch by bending your torso, lifting your shoulder blades several inches off the floor. Exhale at the top. Lower yourself slowly and breathe in.

title: Crunch (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Sit down on the machine. Secure your ankles on the bottom pads, have the middle pad against your lower back, and position your chest on the top pad. Grab the handles for support. Take a breath and flex your abs to push the top pad forward. Exhale. Release as you breathe in.

title: Crunch (Weighted)
howTo: Grab a weight plate and lie on the floor. Position the weight over your chest. Bring your legs together, bend your knees, and plant your feet on the floor. Take a breath and engage your abs to crunch your torso, lifting your shoulder blades several inches off the floor. Breathe out. Lower your torso to the floor as you breathe in.

title: Curtsy Lunge (Dumbbell)
howTo: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand. Engage your abs, bring your shoulders back, and take a breath. Bring your right leg back in a semi-circle, crossing your left leg. Plant the ball of the foot on the floor and descend into a lunge. Descend until your back knee almost touches the floor. Push through your front heel to extend your leg and bring the right leg to its starting position as you exhale. Inhale again and perform the same motion with the opposite leg. Alternate between the left and right leg until you’re done.

title: Dead Bug
howTo: Place an exercise mat on the ground and lie down. Lift your feet off the floor and bend your knees at a 90-degree angle. Lift your arms over your chest. Engage your abs to maintain a spinal curve, and take a breath. Extend your left leg, bringing the foot an inch or two away from the floor. Simultaneously extend your right arm back, bringing the hand close to the floor (optional). Breathe out. Bring your arm and leg to the starting position as you breathe in. Extend your opposite arm and leg.

title: Dead Hang
howTo: Stand underneath a pull-up bar. Reach up and grab the bar with an even overhand grip. Lift your feet off the floor and keep your legs together. Relax your shoulders and midsection, supporting yourself on grip strength. Hang for as long as possible, breathing regularly as you do.

title: Deadlift (Band)
howTo: Grab an open-ended resistance band with handles on both sides. Step over the band, keeping your feet a few inches apart. Straighten your body, bring your shoulders back, and engage your abs. Take a breath and hinge at the hip to lean forward. As you move down, bend your knees to maintain a neutral spine position. Move down as much as you can––ideally, until your hands are several inches off the floor. Hold for a moment and extend your body by driving your hips forward. Exhale near the top.

title: Deadlift (Barbell)
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell. The bar should be over mid-foot, your shins should be an inch or two from the bar, and your feet should be hip-width apart, with toes pointing slightly out. Lean forward and grab the bar with a double overhand grip. Bend your knees to position your shins against the bar. Simultaneously, retract your shoulders to straighten your back. Take a breath. Initiate the pull by digging your heels into the floor. Lift the bar in a straight vertical line, keeping it close to your body. As the bar passes your knees, begin to extend your legs and drive your hips forward to complete the repetition. Begin the descent by driving your buttocks back and keeping your back neutral. Lower the barbell in the same vertical line, exhaling near the bottom.

title: Deadlift (Dumbbell)
howTo: Have your feet hip-width apart, and position a pair of dumbbells parallel to them. When seen from the top, the front weight plate of each dumbbell should align with your mid-foot. Bend forward and grab the dumbbells while keeping your arms straight. Bring your chest out without lifting the dumbbells to put your back into a neutral position. Take a breath and initiate the lift by pressing through your heels as you engage your upper body and legs. Lift the dumbbells in a straight vertical line, moving them to your sides. Extend your hips and knees simultaneously, especially as the dumbbells pass the knees, and continue up until you’re standing. Breathe out at the top. Begin the descent by breaking at the hips and pushing your buttocks back—lower the weights in the same straight line. Breathe in as the weights move down or before initiating the next rep.

title: Deadlift (Smith Machine)
howTo: Set the Smith machine bar in the lowest position and load it appropriately. Stand in front of the bar with your shins a couple of inches away. Lean forward and grab the bar with a double overhand grip. Bend your knees slightly and bring your chest out to put your back in a neutral position. Take a breath and pull the bar up, extending your hips near the top as you breathe out. Hold the top briefly and lower the bar by bringing your buttocks back. Maintain a neutral back position. Breathe in on the way down or before initiating the next rep.

title: Deadlift (Trap bar)
howTo: Load a trap bar and stand inside it. Your feet should be hip-width apart, with the toes pointing slightly out. Squat down and grab the handles on both sides. Bring your shoulders back to straighten your back. Take a breath and initiate the pull by digging your heels into the floor. As the bar moves up, gradually extend your hips and knees until you’re upright. Breathe out. Lower the trap bar slowly. Bring your buttocks back first and gradually bend your knees until you’re at the starting position. Breathe in as the bar moves down or before initiating the next rep.

title: Deadlift High Pull
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell. Spread your feet wide and point your toes out. Have your shins an inch or two from the bar. Lean forward and grab the bar with a double overhand grip. Your hands should be several inches apart. Bend your knees and bring your chest out to position yourself against the bar, and straighten your back. Initiate the pull by digging your heels into the floor and flexing your legs. Extend your hips and knees until you’re upright. Immediately pull the barbell in a straight vertical line as you extend your body. Lift the bar until it reaches chest level and breathe out. Lower the bar and hinge at the hips as the weight reaches your upper thighs. Lower the bar in the same straight vertical line. Breathe in on the way down or before initiating the next rep.

title: Decline Bench Press (Barbell)
howTo: Lie down on the bench, positioning your forehead directly under the bar. Grab the barbell with a double overhand grip. Your arms should be more than shoulder-width apart. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Unrack the bar carefully and bring it in over your chest. Take another breath and lower the barbell to your lower chest. Tap your torso lightly and press the barbell straight up, exhaling near the top. Once finished, bring the barbell over your head and rack it before relaxing your body.

title: Decline Bench Press (Dumbbell)
howTo: Position yourself on a decline bench and secure your feet. Lie back and retract your shoulder blades. Have a friend hand you the dumbbells one by one. Alternatively, hold the dumbbell and lie back carefully. Keep your arms extended, midsection engaged, and weights over your chest. Take a breath. Lower the dumbbells to your sides while keeping your elbows somewhat tucked in. Lower the weights until your elbows are at torso level. Press the weights straight up, exhaling on the way up. Once finished, have someone take at least one dumbbell from your hand so you can sit up.

title: Decline Bench Press (Machine)
howTo: Load the machine appropriately. Set the seat height for the handles to be just below chest level when you sit down. Sit down and grab the handles. Keep your elbows tucked at your sides. Retract your shoulder blades, plant your feet on the floor, squeeze your abs, and inhale. Press the handles forward and down, fully extending your elbows and exhaling. Pause briefly and squeeze your chest. Slowly bend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Decline Bench Press (Smith Machine)
howTo: Position a decline bench underneath a Smith machine. The bench should be positioned so that the bar is above your lower chest when you lie back. Load the bar appropriately and have it at a height where you can reach it from a lying position. Lie back, grab the bar with a double overhand grip, and retract your shoulders. Unrack the bar by extending your arms. Take a breath and lower the bar to your chest. Press the bar to the top position and exhale. Once finished, rack the bar carefully and stand up.

title: Decline Chest Fly (Dumbbell)
howTo: Set the bench at a decline. Grab a pair of light dumbbells and sit on the bench, securing your feet. Keep the dumbbells close to your chest and lie back carefully. Extend your arms, position the dumbbells over your chest, and retract your shoulder blades. Keep your wrists neutral (hands facing one another). Take a breath and bring your arms to your sides, stretching your chest muscles. Keep your elbows slightly bent from start to finish. Bring your arms in, squeeze your chest, and tap the dumbbells at the top as you exhale.

title: Decline Crunch
howTo: Position yourself on a decline bench and secure your feet. Lie back carefully, keeping your spine in contact with the back support. Bring your hands behind your head. Take a breath and crunch your torso by squeezing your rectus abdominis muscle. Lift your shoulder blades several inches off the bench and exhale. Lower your torso as you breathe in.

title: Decline Crunch (Weighted)
howTo: Grab a weight plate, position yourself on a decline bench, and secure your legs. Lie back carefully while keeping the plate against your chest. Take a breath and crunch your torso by engaging your abs. Lift your shoulder blades several inches off the floor and exhale. Hold for a moment and lower your torso slowly as you breathe in.

title: Decline Push Up
howTo: Get down on all fours and position your feet on a gym bench, plyo box, or another sturdy object. Extend your body into a push-up position with your hands wider than shoulder-width apart and rotated slightly out. Have your arms straight and shoulder blades retracted. Engage your abs and take a breath. Lower your body by bending your elbows. Move down until your face is close to the floor. Press yourself back by extending your arms. Exhale near the top.

title: Diamond Push Up
howTo: Get down on all fours and bring your hands together. Your thumbs and index fingers should be in contact or just a couple of inches apart. Extend your body into a push-up position, bringing your shoulders, hips, and knees into alignment. Bring your shoulders back, squeeze your glutes, and take a breath. Lower yourself by bending your elbows. Descend as low as possible and hold the bottom for a moment. Press yourself back to the top, exhaling as you straighten your arms.

title: Downward Dog
howTo: Get down on all fours with your knees underneath your hips. Have your shoulders, elbows, and wrists in vertical alignment. Take a breath and push your buttocks back as you extend your arms. Transition into the high position by elevating your glutes and straightening your legs as you maintain a rigid upper body. Hold the position and release by first bending your knees and then bringing your torso forward.

title: Drag Curl
howTo: Grab a loaded straight bar with your palms facing forward and stand tall. Your hands should be just outside your thighs. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Curl the bar by bending your elbows, dragging the weight against your body. Lift the bar as high as possible, squeezing your biceps at the top and exhaling. Lower the bar in the same straight line as you breathe in.

title: Dragon Flag
howTo: Lie on a flat gym bench and grab the back support with both hands. Extend your body and engage your abs. Take a breath and lift your body off the bench using your midsection musculature. Elevate your body as high as possible and hold for a moment as you exhale. Lower your body slowly and breathe in.

title: Dragonfly
howTo: Lie on a flat gym bench and grab the back support with both hands. Extend your body and engage your abs. Take a breath and lift your body off the bench by engaging your midsection musculature. Elevate your body as high as possible and hold for a moment before exhaling. Lower your body slowly and breathe in.

title: Dumbbell Row
howTo: Grab a dumbbell. Place your right knee and right hand on a flat gym bench. Keep your left leg and arm straight with the dumbbell in your hand. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Row the dumbbell until your elbow is at torso level and squeeze for a moment as you exhale. Lower the weight while keeping your shoulders in position. Breathe in on the way down. Once finished, grab the dumbbell with the opposite hand, position your left hand and knee on the bench, and repeat.

title: Dumbbell Snatch
howTo: Place a dumbbell on the floor. Lean forward and grab the dumbbell with one hand. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Thrust the dumbbell up in one powerful swing. Simultaneously dip slightly (bend your knees) to receive the weight overhead. Extend your arm and straighten your legs as you exhale. Lower the dumbbell slowly, breathing in on the way down. Repeat. Grab the dumbbell with the opposite hand and repeat for the same number of reps.

title: Dumbbell Squeeze Press
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells, sit on a flat gym bench, and place the weights on top of your thighs. Lie back slowly while keeping your arms bent and dumbbells against your body. Extend your arms, position the dumbbells over your chest, have your wrists neutral (palms facing one another), and squeeze the weights together. Bring your shoulders back and inhale. Lower the dumbbells to your chest, hold for a moment, and press them back to the top as you breathe out.

title: Dumbbell Step Up
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand in front of a flat gym bench or plyo box. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Extend one leg forward and plant the foot on the elevated object. Press through your front foot and step up, positioning yourself on the bench or box as you exhale. Step back to the starting position and breathe in. Extend the opposite leg forward. Keep alternating between left and right.

title: Elbow to Knee
howTo: Lie on the floor, bend your knees, rotate your right leg, and position the outer portion of your ankle against your left thigh. Bring your left hand behind your head. Take a breath and engage your abs. Crunch and rotate your torso, bringing your left elbow to your right knee. Exhale. Lower your torso and breathe in. Once finished, place your left ankle against your right thigh and position your right hand behind your head. Repeat for the same number of reps.

title: Elliptical Trainer
howTo: Select the correct resistance and position yourself on the elliptical machine. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Initiate the movement by driving your right leg forward as you pull the right handle back. Maintain a steady tempo and move your arms and legs alternatingly.

title: EZ Bar Biceps Curl
howTo: Load an EZ (curved) bar and grab it with an even underhand grip (palms facing forward). Stand tall with your feet in a comfortable stance, knees bent slightly, and shoulders retracted. Take a breath and lift the bar by engaging your biceps. Move the weight up until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows and breathe out. Pause for a moment and extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Face Pull
howTo: Select a light load, set the pulley to a high position, and attach a rope. Grab the rope with your thumbs facing the ceiling. Take a step back to lift the weight off its stack, bring your shoulders back, and stagger your stance for extra support. Take a breath and pull the rope to your face, splitting it by bringing your arms to your sides. Breathe out. Extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Farmers Walk
howTo: Grab a pair of heavy dumbbells, kettlebells, or weight plates. Stand tall with your shoulders back, your abs tight, and your gaze directed a few feet forward. Walk forward slowly, breathing steadily as you do. Go for as long as possible and bend your knees to set the weights on the floor.

title: Feet Up Bench Press (Barbell)
howTo: Set the bar at a height where you can unrack it without fully extending your arms. Add the appropriate weight and lie on the bench with your forehead directly underneath the bar. Grab the bar evenly with your hands slightly wider than your shoulders. Bring your shoulder blades back and into the bench, and extend your legs. Depending on your height, the back of your thighs, knees, or calves should rest against the edge of the bench. Engage your abs, unrack the bar, and bring it over your mid-chest. Breathe in and lower the barbell to your chest. Pause for a moment and press the bar to the top as you exhale. Breathe in and repeat for the recommended number of reps.

title: Fire Hydrants
howTo: Get down on your hands and knees. Have your knees below your hips and your shoulders, elbows, and wrists in line. Plant your hands flat on the floor and engage your abs to put your back in a neutral position. Flex your right leg without extending the knee. Take a breath and raise your right leg, bringing it to your side. Lift your leg as high as your mobility and strength allow, exhaling at the top. Don’t let your torso rotate with your knee; keep your hips squared. Bring your right leg back to the starting position as you breathe in. Once you’re done, do the same number of reps with the other leg.

title: Floor Press (Barbell)
howTo: Set the barbell on a squat rack at roughly one foot above the floor. Set the squat rack safety bars at torso level (while lying on the floor). Lie flat on the floor with the bar above your forehead. Reach up and grab the bar with an even overhand grip. Retract your shoulder blades, squeeze your abs, and take a breath. Unrack the bar by locking out your elbows. Position the bar over your chest and take another breath. Lower the bar until your elbows come in contact with the floor. Press the bar up, exhaling near the top.

title: Floor Press (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and sit on the floor. Bend your arms to bring the weights close to your torso, and lie back. Keep your knees bent and your feet flat on the floor. Position the dumbbells to your sides, engage your abs, and retract your shoulder blades. Take a breath and press the dumbbells up and in, breathing out at the top. Slowly lower the dumbbells to your sides as you breathe in.

title: Floor Triceps Dip
howTo: Sit down, bend your knees, and place your feet flat on the floor. Lean your torso back and place your hands flat on the floor and behind your body. Engage your abs and take a breath. Press through your palms to extend your arms, lifting your buttocks several inches off the floor. Exhale. Slowly bend your arms, bringing your butt back to the floor as you breathe in.

title: Flutter Kicks
howTo: Lie on the floor with your arms to your sides and hands flat on the floor. Engage your abs and raise your feet a couple of inches off the ground. Breathe in and begin moving your feet up and down alternatingly, several inches in each direction. Maintain a steady exercise tempo and breathing.

title: Frog Jumps
howTo: Stand tall with your feet wider than your hips and toes pointing slightly out. Engage your abs and extend your arms with your hands in front of your hips. Take a breath and descend into a squat, keeping your heels in contact with the floor. Descend until your hands brush the floor. Immediately press through your heels as powerfully as possible, propelling yourself in the air and exhaling at the top. Keep your arms pointing to the floor. Take another breath as you land, descend into the next repetition, and repeat.

title: Frog Pumps (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a dumbbell and lie on the floor. Carefully place the weight on your groin and hold it with both hands. Bring your feet together and have your knees pointed out. Engage your abs and take a breath. Press through your feet to extend your hips toward the ceiling, squeezing your glutes at the top and breathing out. Slowly lower your buttocks to the floor as you breathe in.

title: Front Lever Hold
howTo: Reach up and grab a bar with an even, overhand grip. Your hands should be shoulder-width apart and elbows extended. Bring your feet together and extend your knees by flexing your quadriceps. Maintain a neutral spine by flexing your midsection muscles. Avoid arching your lower back during any part of the front lever. Flex your entire upper body and take a breath. With your body rigid, initiate the front lever by leaning back as you engage your lats and midsection. Raise your entire body horizontally by engaging your back, shoulders, and midsection musculature. Lift your body until you reach a horizontal position and hold, breathing steadily. Slowly move back to the starting position as you relax your body.

title: Front Lever Raise
howTo: Reach up and grab a bar with an even, overhand grip. Your hands should be shoulder-width apart, and your elbows should be extended. Bring your feet together and extend your knees by flexing your quadriceps. Maintain a neutral spine by flexing your midsection muscles. Don't arch your lower back during any part of the front lever. Flex your entire upper body and take a breath. With your body rigid, initiate the front lever by leaning back as you engage your lats and midsection. Raise your entire body in a horizontal position by engaging your back, shoulders, and midsection musculature. Lift your body until you reach a horizontal position, and hold for a moment as you exhale. Slowly move back to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Front Raise (Band)
howTo: Take an open-ended resistance band with handles on both sides. Step over the middle of the band, grab both ends, and position your arms to your sides. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Raise your arms in front of your body in one smooth motion, exhaling at the top. Slowly lower your arms to your sides as you breathe in.

title: Front Raise (Barbell)
howTo: Grab the bar with a double overhand grip (palms facing back). Your hands should be shoulder-width apart. Stand tall, bring your shoulders back, and squeeze your abs. Take a breath and lift the barbell in front of your body, keeping your elbows extended. Lift until the weight is at shoulder level and exhale. Lower the barbell to the starting position, breathing in on the way down.

title: Front Raise (Cable)
howTo: Set the pulley in the lowest position and attach a straight bar. Face away from the cable machine with the bar between your legs. Grab the straight bar with an overhand grip (palms facing back), and have the rope between your legs. Your arms should be straight. Bring your shoulders back, engage your upper body, and bend your knees slightly. Inhale and raise the bar away from your body in one fluid motion. Lift until the bar is at shoulder level and breathe out. Keep your arms straight. Lower your arms to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Front Raise (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Position the dumbbells in front of your thighs with your palms facing back. Bring your shoulders back and inhale. Raise both dumbbells until they are at shoulder level. Exhale at the top. Lower the dumbbells to your upper thighs as you breathe in.

title: Front Raise (Suspension)
howTo: Grab the handles of a suspension trainer with your arms straight and in front of your chest. Lean your body back several degrees, have your feet together, and engage your abs. Take a breath and raise your arms overhead, pulling your body into a vertical position. Exhale. Slowly lower your arms in front of your chest as you lean back and breathe in.

title: Front Squat
howTo: Position the barbell at collarbone height. Dip by bending your knees slightly, pressing your upper chest against the bar, and extending your arms forward. Bend your right elbow and bring your forearm over the bar with the hand resting atop your collarbone. Bend your left elbow and position your left hand on top of your collarbone, right over the bar. Position the barbell on your shoulders, bring your chest out, take a breath, and extend your knees to unrack the bar. Take two steps back to clear enough room. Inhale and descend by bending your knees while keeping your torso upright. Go down as comfortably as possible––ideally, until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Push through your heels to squat up and exhale near the top.

title: Full Squat
howTo: Position a barbell behind your neck and place it on your upper back muscles. Grab the bar evenly for support. Bring your shoulders back and position your feet in a comfortable stance with your toes pointing slightly out. Engage your abs and take a breath. Squat down and keep your heels in contact with the floor. Descend as much as your mobility allows, hold for a moment, and press through your heels to move up. Exhale near the top.

title: Glute Bridge
howTo: Lie flat on the ground. Bend your knees and plant your feet on the floor. Position your arms to your sides for balance. Take a breath and press through your heels to extend your hips toward the ceiling. Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold the position for a moment as you exhale. Lower your buttocks to the floor as you breathe in.

title: Glute Ham Raise
howTo: Set yourself up on a glute ham raise machine. The ankle pad should be against your Achilles heel, and your knees should be on the platform supporting your hips. Push the balls of your feet against the foot platform if your machine has one. With your torso upright, knees bent, and ankles secure, cross your arms in front of your chest, retract your shoulder blades, and inhale. Slowly extend your legs and bring your torso forward and down while keeping your back and hips rigid. Descend as much as possible––ideally, until your knees are almost entirely straight. Engage your posterior chain to bend your knees and raise your torso to the starting position. Exhale near the top.

title: Glute Kickback (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Position your right side to the machine and grab the horizontal support bar. Place the back of your right thigh against the pad, just above the crease of the knee. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and extend your leg back in one fluid motion as you exhale. Slowly bring your leg to the starting position as you breathe in. Once finished, rotate your body 180 degrees, and do the same number of reps for your left leg.

title: Glute Kickback on Floor
howTo: Get down on all fours with your hands flat and knees supporting your lower body. Your knees should be underneath your hips, and your wrists, elbows, and shoulders should be aligned. Engage your abs and take a breath. Kick your right leg back and up, squeezing your glutes at the top and exhale. Keep your knee bent. Slowly bring the leg to the starting position and breathe in. Once finished training one side, do the same number of reps with your other leg.

title: Goblet Squat
howTo: Grab a dumbbell and raise it in front of your torso with both arms while keeping it vertical (with one side up). Position your hands to the sides of it with your palms placed firmly against the top weight plate. Straighten your back, engage your abs, and inhale. Descend into a squat while keeping your torso upright. Squat as low as you comfortably can – ideally until your thighs are parallel with the floor. Push through your heels to raise yourself back to the starting position and exhale near the top of the repetition.

title: Good Morning (Barbell)
howTo: Set the bar up at around armpit height, and grip it evenly, slightly outside shoulder-width level. Tuck yourself under the bar and position it on your lower traps, around the level of your rear deltoids. Extend your upper back and press the bar against it with both hands. Keep your wrist straight and don't rest the bar on your hands. With your feet hip-width apart and firmly planted on the ground, push your hips forward and unrack the bar. Take a few steps back to clear enough space for the lift. With the bar firmly pressed against your upper back and your feet planted into the ground, take a breath and push your hips straight back. As you lean forward, keep your back in a neutral position and think about pushing your butt back. Lean your torso forward as much as possible while keeping your back straight. You should feel an intense stretch in your posterior chain muscles. Allow for a slight bend in your knees at the bottom. Initiate the upward movement by pushing your hips forward and stand tall. Exhale at the top.

title: Gorilla Row (Kettlebell)
howTo: Place two kettlebells of equal weight on the floor and stand before them. Lean forward and grab the two kettlebell handles. Bend your knees to some degree and retract your shoulder blades to straighten your back. Take a deep breath, engage your abs, and row one kettlebell until your elbow is at torso level. Exhale. Lower the weight to the floor as you breathe in. Immediately row the other kettlebell and breathe out. Alternate between left and right until you’re done.

title: Hack Squat
howTo: Place a barbell on the floor and stand in front of it. Your calves should almost be in contact with it. Bring your chest out and descend into a squat. Grab the barbell with your palms facing back, and keep your shoulders retracted. While holding the barbell firmly, take a deep breath and push through your heels to move up. Drag the barbell against your legs and buttocks. Exhale near the top. With your shoulders back and gaze directed forward, bend at the knees and hips to descend.

title: Hack Squat (Machine)
howTo: Add weight to the machine. Position yourself inside the machine with your shoulders against the pad. Have your feet in a comfortable squatting stance on the platform below. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Extend your knees to unrack the weight and remove the safety latch. Inhale and descend by bending your knees. Move down until your knees form a 90-degree angle. Press through your heels and straighten your legs, exhaling near the top. Once finished, put the safety on, rack the weight, and relax your body.

title: Hammer Curl (Band)
howTo: Take a looped or open-ended resistance band, step over the middle, and grab both ends. Stand tall with your arms straight and your wrists neutral (facing your body). Take a breath and slowly bend your arms. Curl the band until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows, and breathe out. Keep your elbows to your sides. Fully extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Hammer Curl (Cable)
howTo: Select the appropriate weight and set the pulley to the lowest position. Attach a rope to the pulley. Lean forward and grab both ends of the rope attachment. Keep your hands neutral (palms facing one another). Stand up and step back to lift the weight off its stack, engage your abs, and retract your shoulder blades. Take a breath and lift the rope to your chest by bending your arms. Keep your elbows at your sides. Bend your arms until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows, and breathe out. Slowly extend your arms and breathe in.

title: Hammer Curl (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Position your arms to your sides with your wrists neutral (palms facing your body). Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Curl both dumbbells simultaneously, lifting them until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows. Exhale. Extend your arms fully as you breathe in.

title: Handstand Hold
howTo: Stand tall with your arms overhead and core engaged. Take a breath and lean forward, planting your hands on the floor. Use the momentum you generate to extend your legs to the ceiling. Hold the handstand position.

title: Handstand Push Up
howTo: Assume a handstand position by placing your hands flat on the floor and lifting your legs to the ceiling. You can support yourself against a wall for extra balance. Once in position, take a breath and lower yourself by bending your elbows. Move down slowly and stop when your head is a couple of inches from the floor. Press by extending your elbows and exhale.

title: Hang Clean
howTo: Stand tall and hold a barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing back). Your hands should be more than shoulder-width apart. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and position your feet comfortably. Take a breath and thrust the barbell forward and up. Immediately sink into a squat and receive the barbell in a front rack position. Your elbows should be bent, and your fingertips should support the barbell as it lies on your shoulders. Press through your heels and extend your knees to stand up, exhaling at the top. Grab hold of the barbell and carefully lower it to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Hang Snatch
howTo: Grab a loaded barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing back). Stand tall and straighten your arms. Engage your abs and take a breath. Hinge at the hips until the barbell is just above knee level. Drive your hips forward as you thrust the weight overhead, bending your knees and sinking into a squat. Catch the bar overhead with your elbows extended. Push through your heels and squat up while keeping your arms straight. Exhale at the top.

title: Hanging Knee Raise
howTo: Reach up and grab a pull-up bar with an overhand grip (palms facing forward). Your hands should be shoulder-width apart. Bring your legs together, engage your abs, and retract your shoulders. Take a breath and lift your legs, bending your knees. Raise your legs as high as possible in one fluid motion and exhale. Slowly extend your legs as you breathe in. Don’t let your lower back arch.

title: Hanging Leg Raise
howTo: Reach up and grab a pull-up bar with an even overhand grip (palms facing forward). Take a breath, engage your abs, and lift your feet off the floor, suspending yourself in the air. Raise your legs to your torso and simultaneously bend your knees. Lift your legs as high as possible in one fluid motion and exhale. Slowly extend and lower your legs while keeping your body stable as you breathe in.

title: Heel Taps
howTo: Lie on the floor, bend your knees, bring your feet together, and plant them flat. Position your arms to your sides and take a breath. Crunch your torso to the left, tapping your left ankle with your hand. Extend your upper body, take another breath, and crunch to the right. Alternate between left and right until you’re done.

title: Hex Press (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells, preferably hexagonal ones. Sit on a flat gym bench and place the weights on your thighs. Lie back while keeping the weights close to your torso. As you lie down, extend your arms and squeeze the dumbbells together. Retract your shoulder blades, take a deep breath, and squeeze your abs. Lower the dumbbells to your upper stomach and pause briefly. Press the weights to extend your arms and exhale.

title: High Knee Skips
howTo: Stand tall with your arms to your sides, legs straight, and feet a few inches apart. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and inhale. Raise your left knee toward your chest as you bring your left arm back. Simultaneously bend your right arm, positioning your hand to the side of your face. Lower your left foot to the floor as you exhale, and bring your right arm to your side. Inhale and immediately bring the opposite knee to your chest, bending your left arm. Alternate and maintain a steady tempo from start to finish.

title: High Knees
howTo: Stand tall with your shoulders back, abs engaged, and gaze forward. Take a breath and raise your right knee to your chest while simultaneously bringing your right arm behind your body. Lower your knee and immediately lift the opposite one, bringing your left arm behind your body. Raise your knees to your chest alternatingly, maintaining a steady tempo as you do.

title: Hip Abduction (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load and sit down. Bring your shoulders back and grab the handles to your sides. Step on the foot platforms and position the outer part of your lower thighs against the pads. Take a breath and spread your legs out as much as possible in one fluid motion, breathing out. Bring your knees together as you breathe in.

title: Hip Adduction (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Sit down and place your inner thighs against the machine pads. Position your feet on the foot platforms below. Grab the handles to your sides, retract your shoulder blades, and take a breath. Squeeze your inner thigh muscles to bring the pads together as you breathe out. Move your legs to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Hip Thrust
howTo: Sit on the floor and position your upper back against the edge of a bench. Bend your arms and place your hands on your chest. Bend your knees and plant your feet flat on the floor. Your knees should point forward, not to the sides. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Engage your glutes and push through your heels to raise your butt until your hips align with your shoulders. Hold the contraction for a moment and exhale. Lower your butt to the floor as you breathe in.

title: Hip Thrust (Barbell)
howTo: Sit on the floor and position a loaded barbell over your hips. Place your upper back against the edge of a flat gym bench. Bend your knees and plant your feet flat on the floor. Place your hands over the barbell and grab it firmly. Engage your abs and take a breath. Thrust the barbell toward the ceiling by squeezing your buttocks and pushing through your heels. Exhale at the top. Lower your hips to the floor and breathe in.

title: Hip Thrust (Machine)
howTo: Select or load the appropriate weight. Lie back on the machine with your arms to your sides and your upper back in contact with the pad. Secure the strap or belt over your hips. Take a deep breath and engage your abs. Press through your heels to extend your hips toward the ceiling. Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold for a moment as you exhale. Slowly lower your buttocks to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Hip Thrust (Smith Machine)
howTo: Set the Smith machine bar to a low position, enough to be able to tuck underneath. Load the bar appropriately and place a pad to prevent hip discomfort. Position a flat gym bench parallel or perpendicular to the bar at an approximately one-foot distance (tweak the setup to find your ideal position). Sit on the floor and lean back to position your shoulder blades on the bench. The bar should be directly over your hips. Bend your knees and plant your feet on the floor. Grab the bar, push your hips against the pad, and engage your abs. Breathe in and push through your heels to raise your hips toward the ceiling until they align with your knees and upper body. Exhale at the top. Slowly lower your buttocks to the floor as you breathe in.

title: Hollow Rock
howTo: Lie down on an exercise mat. Have your hands at your sides, feet together, and legs straight. Contract your abs as if trying to initiate a crunch. Doing so will put your lower back against the exercise mat. Squeeze your thigh muscles as your legs are together, and lift your feet a couple of inches off the floor. Keep your legs straight. Lift your head a couple of inches off the floor and extend your arms back behind your head. Hold the position for as long as possible and breathe steadily.

title: Incline Bench Press (Barbell)
howTo: Lie back on the incline bench and grab the bar with an even overhand grip. The bar should be close enough to unrack without overextending your body. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and plant your feet on the floor. Take a breath and unrack the bar by locking out your elbows. Bring the barbell over your chest and take another breath. Lower the weight to your mid chest and pause for a moment. Press the bar in a straight line and exhale near the top. Once finished, carefully bring the bar over your head and rack it before relaxing.

title: Incline Bench Press (Dumbbell)
