howTo: Set the incline of a bench at 45 degrees, grab a pair of dumbbells, and sit down. Place the dumbbells on top of your thighs, and lie back as you kick the weights up and over your torso. Bring the dumbbells to your sides, plant your feet on the floor, retract your shoulders, and take a breath. Keep your elbows somewhat tucked in. Press both dumbbells toward the ceiling, tapping them lightly at the top. Breathe out. Lower the weights to your sides, breathing in on the way down.

title: Incline Bench Press (Smith Machine)
howTo: Place an incline bench under the Smith machine. The bar should be directly over your mid-chest when you lie down. Set the bar at a height you can reach without fully extending your arms. Lie down, grab the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing forward), and dig your shoulder blades into the bench. Engage your abs and press the bar up, rotating it to remove the safety. Take a breath and lower the bar to your chest. Press it back to the top and exhale. Once finished, rack the bar.

title: Incline Chest Fly (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and sit at the base of an incline gym bench. Place the weights on top of your thighs and lie back slowly. Lift the dumbbells over your chest and extend your arms. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Extend your arms to your sides until your elbows are at torso level. Keep your elbows slightly bent. Bring your arms in, squeezing your chest, and tap the dumbbells at the top. Exhale.

title: Incline Chest Press (Machine)
howTo: Add the appropriate amount of weight. Adjust the seat height so you can comfortably grab the handles and keep your elbows tucked as you sit down. Sit down, plant your feet on the floor, and grab the handles. Retract your shoulder blades, engage your abs, and take a breath. Press the handles up and overhead in one fluid motion, exhaling at the top. Slowly bend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Incline Push Ups
howTo: Place your hands on the edge of a sturdy object (plyo box, gym bench, or a chair). Straighten your body as you would for a regular push-up. Bring your shoulders back and squeeze your glutes. Take a breath and lower yourself to the object you’re using, tapping your chest at the bottom position. Press yourself back to the top and fully extend your arms as you exhale.

title: Inverted Row
howTo: Set a bar for the rows at around hip height. You can use a Smith machine. Grab the bar with an overhand grip and tuck yourself underneath. With your arms straight and bar over your chest, extend your body and balance your lower half on your heels. Engage your glutes and abs, bring your chest out, and take a breath. Your body should be as straight as an arrow. Pull through your elbows to bring your body toward the bar. Touch the bar to your chest without flaring your elbows. Breathe out. Slowly lower yourself to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Iso-Lateral Chest Press (Machine)
howTo: Add the appropriate weight to the machine. Adjust the seat height. You should be able to press without flaring your elbows. Sit down and grab the handles with an overhand grip (palms facing down). Bring your shoulders back, plant your feet on the floor, and take a breath. Press the handles forward and up, exhaling as your arms straighten. Bend your elbows as you breathe in.

title: Iso-Lateral High Row (Machine)
howTo: Load the machine and set the thigh pad for your legs to fit snugly. Sit down and grab the handles. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Pull the handles to your torso, squeezing your back muscles, and breathe out. Slowly extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Iso-Lateral Low Row
howTo: Add the appropriate amount of weight. Sit down with your stomach and chest against the pad. Reach forward and grab the two handles. Retract your shoulder blades, take a deep breath, and engage your abs. Pull both handles until your elbows are at your sides, and squeeze your back muscles as you exhale. Slowly extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Iso-Lateral Row (Machine)
howTo: Add the appropriate weight to the machine. Adjust the seat height to have your torso against the front pad when you sit down. Sit down, plant your feet flat, position your torso against the pad, and grab the handles. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Pull the handles to your sides, squeezing your back at the top of the repetition and exhaling. Slowly extend your arms while keeping your shoulders retracted and breathe in.

title: Jack Knife (Suspension)
howTo: Secure your feet on the handles of a suspension kit. Extend your body into a push-up position with your arms straight, shoulders back, and abs tight. Take a breath and tuck your knees, bringing them close to your elbows. Breathe out. Slowly extend your knees as you breathe in.

title: Jackknife Sit Up
howTo: Lie flat on the floor with your legs straight and arms at your sides. Your heels, legs, butt, upper back, head, and arms should be in contact with the floor. Engage your abs and raise your legs as you bend your knees. Simultaneously lift your torso, bringing your arms toward your lower body. Exhale. Extend your body back to the starting position and breathe in on the way down.

title: JM Press (Barbell)
howTo: Set the barbell at a height where you can reach it without fully extending your arms. Lie on the bench, reach up, grab the bar with a shoulder-width grip, and unrack it. Position the bar over your chest with your arms straight, just as you would during a standard bench press. Plant your feet on the floor, retract your shoulder blades, take a deep breath, and engage your abs. Slowly bend your elbows, lowering the barbell to your upper chest. Pause briefly and fully extend your arms as you exhale.

title: Jump Shrug
howTo: Grab a barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing back) and stand tall. Your hands should be just outside your thighs. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Lean your torso forward slightly by hinging at the hip. Take a breath, bend your knees slightly, and press through your heels to jump. Immediately shrug your shoulders as high as possible and exhale.

title: Jump Squat
howTo: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointing slightly out. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and position your hands in front of your torso. Take a breath and descend halfway into a squat. Press through your heels to move up and immediately jump as high as possible, swinging your arms to your sides and behind your body as you exhale. Take another breath as you land and descend into another squat.

title: Jumping Jack
howTo: Stand tall with your arms to your sides, legs straight, and feet together. Engage your abs and take a breath. Hop and bring your feet out as you extend your arms to your sides and over your head. Jump again and bring your feet together and arms to your sides. Exhale. Keep repeating rhythmically, hopping just a couple of inches off the floor, and breathing regularly.

title: Jumping Lunge
howTo: Stand tall with your feet a few inches apart, arms to your sides, and shoulders back. Engage your abs and take a breath. Jump vertically and immediately bring your right leg forward and your left leg back. As you land on your front foot and the ball of your left foot, immediately sink into a lunge. Descend until your front thigh is almost parallel to the floor. Press through your front heels to move up and immediately jump. Exhale as you start moving up. Switch leg positions while in the air, breathe in, and sink into another lunge. Keep jumping and switching leg positions, doing a lunge in between.

title: Kettlebell Around the World
howTo: Hold a kettlebell in your right hand and stand tall with your feet hip-width apart and shoulders back. Engage your abs and breathe in. Move the kettlebell to the left in front of your body and switch hands, grabbing the weight with your left hand. Continue the rotation by moving the kettlebell back and around your body. As the kettlebell gets to your buttocks, reach back with your right hand and grab it, continuing the rotation in the same direction. Continue switching hands as the kettlebell rotates around your waist and breathe steadily. Maintain a consistent tempo and keep your abs tense. Once finished rotating the weight in one direction, do the same number of reps in the opposite direction.

title: Kettlebell Clean
howTo: Position the kettlebell on the floor. Stand in front of the weight with your feet hip-width apart. Bend your knees and lean your torso forward, grabbing the kettlebell with your right hand. Engage your abs and take a breath. Press through your heels to straighten your knees as you simultaneously pull the weight in a straight vertical line close to your body. As the kettlebell reaches hip level, drive your hips forward and thrust the weight up. Bend your arm and have the kettlebell land on your shoulder, lying against the top of your forearm and biceps. Exhale. Lower the weight to the floor and breathe in on the way down or before initiating the next rep.. Once finished, grab the kettlebell with your left hand and do the same number of reps.

title: Kettlebell Curl
howTo: Grab a pair of kettlebells and position them to your sides with your arms straight and palms facing forward. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Take a breath and curl the kettlebells, bending your arms until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows. Breathe out at the top. Slowly extend your arms, breathing in as you lower them. Repeat.

title: Kettlebell Goblet Squat
howTo: Grab a kettlebell by the horns––both sides of the handle with palms facing one another. Lift the weight in front of your chest with your elbows bent. Bring your shoulders back and position your feet in a comfortable stance with your toes pointing slightly out. Take a breath and engage your abs. Descend by bending your knees and maintaining an upright torso as you move down. Squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor and your elbows are between your thighs. Hold the bottom position for a moment. Push through your heels to move up, keeping the kettlebell close to your body. Exhale as you straighten your legs.

title: Kettlebell Halo
howTo: Grab a kettlebell by the horns with your wrists neutral and thumbs facing forward. Stand up and turn the kettlebell bottoms up with your palms now facing your body and your elbows bent at roughly 90 degrees. Assume a comfortable stance, bring your chest out, and engage your abs. Breathe in and pull the kettlebell back and around the right side of your head. This will cause your left forearm to travel over your head. As the kettlebell moves behind your head, continue the rotation by raising your right forearm above your head. Complete the rep by bringing the kettlebell to the starting position in front of your chest. Exhale. Breathe in and rotate the kettlebell around your head, this time in the opposite direction.

title: Kettlebell High Pull
howTo: Position a kettlebell on the floor. Spread your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, with your toes pointing out. Bend your knees and grab the kettlebell handle with both hands. Keep your back neutral. Take a breath and press through your heels to extend your arms. As the kettlebell reaches hip level, use the momentum and pull the weight vertically into an upright row, exhaling at the top. Slowly extend your arms and bend your knees and hips to set the weight on the floor, inhaling on the way down or before initiating the next rep.

title: Kettlebell Shoulder Press
howTo: Grab a kettlebell and lift it to shoulder level. Keep your elbow bent and the weight positioned against the top of your forearm and bicep. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and have your feet in a comfortable position. Take a breath and press the weight straight up, fully extending your arm and exhaling. Slowly lower it to your side as you breathe in. Repeat. Once finished, grab the kettlebell with your opposite hand, performing the same number of reps.

title: Kettlebell Snatch
howTo: Position a kettlebell in front of your body. Have your feet shoulder-width apart, with your toes pointing slightly out. Bend your knees and lean forward to grab the kettlebell. Stand tall and hold the weight in front of your body. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and breathe in. Hinge at the hips as you bring the kettlebell back and between your legs. Extend your knees and hips to generate momentum and raise the weight forward and up, lifting it overhead in one smooth motion. Fully straighten your arm as you lift the kettlebell overhead and exhale. Lower the kettlebell in a straight line as you breathe in. Repeat. Once finished, do the same number of reps with your opposite arm.

title: Kettlebell Swing
howTo: Grab a kettlebell by its handle with your wrists neutral (palms facing one another). Stand tall and have the weight in front of your hips. Your feet should be shoulder-width apart or slightly wider. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Hinge at the hip, bringing your buttocks back. The kettlebell should end up between your thighs. Extend your hips forcefully, thrusting the kettlebell in front of your body until it ends up at shoulder height, and exhale. Lower the kettlebell and immediately hinge at the hips again for the weight to end up between your thighs.

title: Kettlebell Turkish Get Up
howTo: Lie on the floor with your right knee bent and your right foot flat on the ground. Have your left leg straight. Hold a kettlebell in your right hand and keep your wrist, elbow, and shoulder in vertical alignment. Move your left arm to the side with the hand flat on the floor. Engage your abs, take a breath, and raise your torso off the floor. Keep your right arm straight and the kettlebell over your shoulder joint. Push yourself with your left arm until your torso is vertical and you're seated. With the kettlebell still over your right shoulder and left hand supporting you, bend your left leg and place the knee on the floor, raising yourself. Raise your torso higher and remove your left hand from the floor. Square your hips to create a solid lunge position, take another breath, and lift your left knee, stepping on the left foot. Get up to the standing position by extending your knees and hips. Keep the kettlebell over your shoulder, and don’t bend your elbow. Reverse the motion by lunging back, lifting your left foot off the floor, landing on your left knee, and rotating the left leg behind your body. Place your left hand on the floor, extend your left leg in front of you, and get into the seated position without extending the right knee. Bend your left arm to lower your torso and lie on your back and into the starting position.

title: Kipping Pull Up
howTo: Reach up and grab the pull-up bar with an overhand grip (palms facing forward). Raise your legs slightly and bring your buttocks back to swing your body slightly and generate momentum. Use the momentum you generate by allowing your legs to swing back, bringing your back into hyperextension. Continue building momentum by allowing your legs to swing forward. Bend your elbows to pull yourself up. Bring your chin over the bar, straighten your body, and allow the momentum to til you slightly back, similar to an inverted row. Lower yourself immediately and keep your body in the tilted position. Once your arms are almost entirely straight, allow your legs to drop and swing back as your buttocks travel back, causing an arch in your spine. Swing your legs forward again and use the momentum to pull yourself up a second time. Keep moving up and down, using momentum to go from back flexion to hyperextension on each repetition.

title: Knee Raise Parallel Bars
howTo: Position yourself inside a captain’s chair with your feet on a platform or steps, back against the pad, and forearms flat on the pair of padded parallel bars. Bring your shoulders back and contract your abs. Step off the platform or steps and support yourself with your upper body musculature. Keep your shoulders in position and avoid slumping. Take a breath and engage your abs to raise your knees as high as possible in one fluid motion. Breathe out at the top. Lower your knees to the starting position, extending them on the way down, and stop before your lower back begins to arch. Breathe in on the way down.

title: Kneeling Pulldown (band)
howTo: Attach a resistance band overhead. Grab both resistance band handles, step back, and kneel. Lean your torso forward in the direction of the resistance band anchor point. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Pull the band to your shoulders while keeping your elbows tucked in and in line with your torso. Exhale. Extend your arms slowly as you breathe in.

title: Kneeling Push Up
howTo: Get down on your hands and knees. Lean forward and position your hands in a comfortable stance. Engage your abs and take a breath. Descend by bending your elbows. Move down until your face is an inch from the floor and pause. Press yourself back to the top and exhale.

title: L-Sit Hold
howTo: Position yourself on a captain’s chair or parallel dip bars. Grab the handles evenly and straighten your arms. Suspend yourself in the air and engage your abs. Take a breath and raise your legs until they are parallel to the floor. Hold the position for 15 to 60 seconds and release.

title: Landmine 180
howTo: Secure one end of a barbell on a landmine attachment and put a weight plate on the other. Grab the barbell sleeve with your palms supporting the bottom end. Stand tall and position the barbell to your right with your arms straight and abs flexed. Take a breath and raise the barbell to your face by bending your elbows. Immediately lower the weight to your left, extending your arms. Exhale. Take another breath and lift the barbell in the opposite direction.

title: Landmine Row
howTo: Secure one end of a barbell to a landmine attachment. Add the appropriate amount of weight to the other side of the bar. Take a V-handle and position it underneath the barbell. Grab the handle with both hands, placing it securely against the end of the barbell shaft (just before the sleeve begins). Bend your knees, retract your shoulder blades, and squeeze your abs. Lift the barbell a couple of inches off the floor and take another breath. Row the bar to your upper stomach and pause briefly as you exhale. Lower the barbell by extending your arms and breathe in. Don’t rest the bar on the floor.

title: Landmine Squat and Press
howTo: Anchor one end of a barbell on a landmine and add a weight plate on the other. Reach down and grab the free barbell end with both hands. Straighten your body and have your hands together and in front of your chest. Position your feet in a comfortable stance for squats. Engage your abs, take a breath, and descend into a squat. Go down until your thighs are parallel to the floor, and press through your heels to move back up. As you straighten your legs, immediately press the bar overhead and exhale at the top. Slowly lower the bar to your chest, inhale, and squat.

title: Lat Pulldown (Band)
howTo: Anchor a resistance band overhead - for example, on a pull-up bar. Grab both ends of the resistance band, straighten your arms, step back to lengthen the band, and sit on a plyo box or gym bench. You can also kneel. Lean slightly in the direction of the band anchor point. Take a breath and pull the band to your upper chest, squeezing your lats at the bottom and breathing out. Extend your arms slowly and breathe in.

title: Lat Pulldown (Cable)
howTo: Adjust the knee pad on the machine to be right against your thighs. Select a weight you can lift for at least ten smooth reps. Grab the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Your palms should face forward. Sit down and secure your legs underneath the pad. With your arms extended, bring your shoulders back and down. Take a breath and pull the weight down through your elbows. As you pull, keep your elbows tucked and in line with your torso. Breathe out. Hold for a moment and extend your arms fully as you breathe in.

title: Lat Pulldown (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Adjust the thigh pad. Your legs should fit snugly. Stand up and grab the handles with an even overhand grip (palms facing forward). Keep your arms straight and sit down, securing your legs under the pad. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Pull the handles down, tucking your elbows and bringing them in line with your torso. Breathe out. Extend your arms slowly and breathe in.

title: Lat Pulldown - Close Grip (Cable)
howTo: Attach a V handle to a lat pulldown machine. Select the appropriate load. Adjust the thigh pad for your legs to fit snugly underneath. Grab the V handle with a neutral grip (palms facing one another), sit down, and position your thighs underneath the pad. Bring your shoulders back and lean your torso slightly back. Inhale and pull the V handle to your chest, squeezing your back muscles and exhaling. Slowly extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Lateral Band Walks
howTo: Wrap a looped resistance band around your ankles or thighs, just above the knees. Lean your torso forward a bit and bend your knees slightly. Engage your abs and take a breath. Take a lateral step to the right and exhale. Inhale again and take another step in the same direction. Once finished walking in one direction, take the same number of steps to the left, breathing steadily as you do.

title: Lateral Box Jump
howTo: Stand sideways to a plyometric box or flat gym bench. Have your feet close, arms to your sides, and your body straight. Take a breath, descend into a quarter squat, and swing your arms back. Press through your heels to extend your legs and drive your arms forward, jumping laterally and up. Breathe out. Land on the elevated platform and carefully get down on the opposite side as you breathe in. Jump laterally in the opposite direction. Keep alternating.

title: Lateral Leg Raises
howTo: Stand tall with your feet together, legs straight, and shoulders back. Grab onto something for balance––for example, a squat rack or chair. Engage your abs and take a breath. Raise your right leg laterally as high as possible in one fluid motion and exhale. Lower your leg to the starting position and breathe in. Once finished, do the same number of reps with the opposite leg.

title: Lateral Lunge
howTo: Stand tall with your feet a few inches apart and your toes pointing slightly out. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and position your hands in front of your chest. Take a breath and bring your left leg to the side, planting the foot flat on the floor. Immediately sink into a lunge by bending the knee. Keep your opposite leg straight and your foot on the ground. Descend until your left thigh is almost parallel to the floor and hold for a moment. Press through your left heel to extend your knee and bring your leg to the starting position as you exhale. Inhale again as you bring your right leg out, performing the same movement. Keep alternating.

title: Lateral Raise (Band)
howTo: Take an open-ended resistance band with handles on both ends. Step over the middle of the band and hold the handles to your sides. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and lean your torso slightly forward. Your hands should be in front of your hips. Raise your arms to your sides in one fluid motion. Go up until your shoulders, elbows, and wrists align, exhaling at the top. Lower your arms to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Lateral Raise (Cable)
howTo: Set the pulleys of a double cable machine in the lowest position and attach handles. Select the appropriate weight on both stacks. Walk to the left and grab the handle with your right hand. Walk to the right and grab the other handle with your left hand. Stand in the middle with your arms straight and your hands in front of your hips. Engage your abs, retract your shoulder blades, and breathe in. Raise both arms to your sides in one fluid motion, going up until your shoulders, elbows, and wrists align. Exhale at the top. Lower your arms as you breathe in.

title: Lateral Raise (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of light dumbbells and stand tall. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Lift both arms laterally until your elbows and wrists align with your shoulders. Exhale at the top. Lower the dumbbells to your sides as you breathe in.

title: Lateral Raise (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load and adjust the seat’s height. Sit down and bring your shoulders back and into the back support. Grab the handles on your sides and position your forearms flat against the pads. Take a breath and bring your arms to your sides smoothly, going up until your elbows are at shoulder level. Breathe out. Lower your arms to your sides and breathe in.

title: Lateral Squat
howTo: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, and point your toes slightly out. Raise your arms in front of your body for balance. Breathe in, bring your left leg out several inches, plant your foot, and squat. Go down as low as your strength and mobility allow. Hold for a moment and forcefully push through your heels to bring yourself to the top as you exhale. Breathe in, bring your right leg out, plant your foot, and squat. Keep alternating.

title: Leg Extension (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Adjust the pad to be against your lower legs, just above your feet when seated. Sit down, grab the handles by your sides, place your lower shins against the pad, and retract your shoulders. Take a breath and straighten your legs by engaging your quadriceps. Lift the weight until your knees straighten, and breathe out. Hold for a moment and bend your knees slowly as you breathe in.

title: Leg Press (Machine)
howTo: Add the appropriate weight to the leg press machine and sit down. Lift your legs and plant your feet flat on the platform. Have your feet in a comfortable position with your toes pointing slightly out. Grab the handles to your sides, bring your shoulders back, and engage your abs. Press the platform and straighten your legs while rotating the handles to remove the safety pins. Take another breath and lower the platform by bending your knees. Hold the bottom position for a moment and press the platform away as you exhale. Once finished, straighten your legs, rotate the handles to put the safety pins on, and rest.

title: Leg Press Horizontal (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load and sit down. Place your feet flat on the platform at a comfortable width. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and grab the handles by your sides. Take a breath and press the platform forward until you straighten your legs. Exhale. Bend your legs slowly as you breathe in.

title: Leg Raise Parallel Bars
howTo: Position yourself on a captain’s chair. Have your forearms flat against the horizontal pads and grab the handles for support. Bring your feet together and engage your abs to position your lower back against the back support. Take a breath and raise your legs in one fluid motion, going as high as possible. Exhale at the top. Slowly lower your legs to the starting position and keep the tension on your abs. Breathe in on the way down, and don’t let your lower back arch.

title: Low Cable Fly Crossovers
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Set the pulleys in the lowest position and attach handles. Grab both handles one at a time and stand in the middle with your arms to your sides. Stagger your stance, bring your shoulders back, and squeeze your abs. Take a breath and bring your arms up and in, tapping the handles lightly as you squeeze your chest and breathe out. Slowly bring your arms to your sides as you breathe in.

title: Low Row (Suspension)
howTo: Grab the handles of a suspension kit and extend your arms in front of your body. Step forward a bit to lean your body at a comfortable angle. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and squeeze your glutes. Take a breath and pull yourself up, squeezing your back muscles at the top and exhaling. Slowly extend your arms and breathe in.

title: Lunge
howTo: Stand tall with your shoulders back, hands on your hips, and gaze directed forward. Take a breath and engage your abs. Lunge forward, bringing your right leg in front of your body, and plant the foot on the floor. Immediately descend by bending your knee and going down until your back knee taps the floor. Press through your front heel to stand up and exhale. Bring your back foot forward, plant it on the floor, and lunge again.

title: Lunge (Barbell)
howTo: Position a barbell on your upper back and grab it for support. Have your feet hip-width apart with your toes pointing slightly out. Take a breath, lunge forward, and plant your foot flat on the floor. Immediately sink into a lunge while keeping your torso stable and upright. Descend until your back knee is an inch or two from the floor. Press through your front heel to extend your knee and bring the leg to the starting position as you exhale. Take another breath and lunge forward, extending the opposite leg. Keep alternating.

title: Lunge (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells, hold them to your sides, and stand tall. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and have your feet in a comfortable stance. Take a breath and lunge forward, plating one foot in front of your body. Immediately sink into a lunge and descend until your front thigh is almost parallel to the floor. Your back knee should be an inch or two from the floor. Press through your front heel to extend your leg and bring it back to the starting position as you exhale. Take another breath and lunge again, bringing your opposite leg forward. Keep alternating.

title: Lying Knee Raise
howTo: Lie on the floor with your feet together, arms to your sides, and your body straight. Engage your abs and lift your feet an inch or two from the floor. Your lower back should be in contact with the floor. Take a breath and lift your legs, bending your knees at a 90-degree angle. Breathe out. Slowly lower your legs to the starting position as you straighten your knees. Breathe in and keep your feet off the floor.

title: Lying Leg Curl (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load and adjust the pad to be over your Achilles tendon (above the heel) when you lie down. Lie down, grab the handles, and place the back of your lower legs against the pad. Take a breath and engage your hamstrings to curl your legs, lifting the weight. Curl until your lower legs are almost vertical and exhale at the top. Hold for a moment and extend your knees slowly as you inhale.

title: Lying Leg Raise
howTo: Lie on the floor with your legs straight, arms to your sides, and palms flat on the floor. Engage your abs, take a breath, and bring your legs together. Lift your legs in the air until your heels point at the ceiling. Breathe out. Lower your legs slowly without resting your feet on the floor and breathe in. Maintain slight roundness in your lower back throughout each repetition.

title: Lying Neck Curls
howTo: Lie face up on a flat gym bench with your head over the edge. Breathe in and lower your head to a comfortable level. Flex the muscles on the front of your neck to perform a repetition as you exhale. Lower your head again as you breathe in.

title: Lying Neck Curls (Weighted)
howTo: Grab a weight plate and lie face up on a flat gym bench with your head over the edge. Position the weight plate against your forehead and hold it with both hands. Breathe in and lower your head to a comfortable level. Flex the muscles on the front of your neck to raise your head as you exhale.

title: Lying Neck Extension
howTo: Lie face down on a flat gym bench with your head over the edge. Grab the back support for balance or place your hands on the ground. Inhale and extend your neck back in one fluid motion. Exhale. Lower your head as you breathe in, and repeat.

title: Lying Neck Extension (Weighted)
howTo: Grab a weight plate and lie face down on a flat gym bench with your head over the edge. Position the weight behind your head and hold it with both hands. Inhale and lower your head until you feel a stretch at the back of your neck. Extend your neck in one fluid motion to lift your head and exhale.

title: Meadows Rows (Barbell)
howTo: Set a barbell on a landmine attachment or place one end of the bar at a wall angle to serve as an anchor point. Load the opposite side of the barbell with some weight. Stand perpendicular to the loaded side of the bar (one side facing it). Assume a staggered stance, bend forward, and grab the barbell sleeve with the hand closer to the bar. Raise your torso enough for the loaded side of the barbell to lift an inch or two off the floor while your arm is straight. Bring your shoulders back, take a breath, and pull the bar up and close to your side, squeezing your lat. Pull the weight until your elbow is at a 90-degree angle and the barbell sleeve is at torso level as you exhale. Extend your arm slowly, breathing in on the way down. Don’t protract your shoulder (roll it forward) as you reach the bottom of each rep. Once finished training one side, turn your body 180 degrees and do the same number of reps with your opposite arm.

title: Mountain Climber
howTo: Get down on all fours. Have your hands and knees flat on the floor. Straighten your leg to support your lower body on your toes. Engage your abs and lift your buttocks several inches toward the ceiling. Take a breath and tuck your right knee to your chest in one fluid motion. Exhale. Extend the right leg as you breathe in. Tuck your left knee to your chest and exhale. Keep alternating and maintain a steady tempo from start to finish.

title: Muscle Up
howTo: Stand underneath a pull-up bar, reach up, jump, and grab it with a double overhand grip (palms facing forward). As you grab the bar, engage your abs, have your shoulder blades back, and keep your hips slightly flexed, legs straight, and feet together. As your body beings to swing back, pull yourself up as powerfully as possible, aiming to bring your body back and around the pull-up bar. The momentum should carry you high enough for your chest to end up slightly above the bar. Perform a straight bar dip to extend your elbows, exhaling near the top. Lower yourself by bending your elbows and moving your body down from the bar. Swing back and forth to create momentum and begin the next repetition just as your body starts swinging back.

title: Negative Pull Up
howTo: Place a stool, gym bench, or plyo box underneath a pull-up bar. Step on the object and grab the pull-up bar with an overhand grip (palms facing forward). Your elbows should be bent at roughly 90 degrees. Take a deep breath and engage your abs. Step off the object to suspend yourself in the air and slowly extend your arms against the force of gravity. Once done, step on the sturdy object again and repeat.

title: Nordic Hamstrings Curls
howTo: Get down on your knees and secure your feet underneath a loaded barbell or another sturdy object. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and squeeze your glutes. You can have your arms to your sides or position your hands in front of your torso for support. Take a breath and lower your torso to the floor by slowly extending your knees. Keep your hips stable and your glutes engaged. Lower yourself as much as your strength allows and engage your posterior muscles to lift your torso to an upright position. Exhale near the top.

title: Oblique Crunch
howTo: Lie on your right side, bend your knees, and stack your legs. Place your left hand behind your head. Take a breath and engage your top obliques to crunch your torso, lifting your right shoulder off the floor. Breathe out. Lower your shoulder to the floor as you breathe in. Repeat. Once finished, lie on your left side and repeat for the same number of reps.

title: One Arm Push Up
howTo: Get down on all fours. Extend your legs and support your lower body on your toes. Spread your feet wider than hip-width distance. Have your hands shoulder-width apart or slightly wider. Engage your abs. With your body tense and in position, lift one hand off the floor and bring it back behind your back. Take a breath and lower yourself by bending your supporting arm. Go down as much as you can––ideally, until your chest is a couple of inches from the floor. Extend your arm to get back to the starting position and exhale. Once finished on one side, support your body on your other hand and do the same number of reps.

title: Overhead Curl (Cable)
howTo: Set the pulleys of a double cable machine in the highest position. Select the appropriate weight on both stacks and attach handles to the pulleys. Grab the two handles and stand in the middle of the cable machine with your arms straight and to your sides. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Simultaneously bend your arms, bringing the handles to the sides of your head as you breathe out. Slowly extend your arms as you inhale.

title: Overhead Dumbbell Lunge
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Raise the weights overhead and straighten your arms. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Extend your right leg forward, planting your foot on the floor. Descend into a lunge until your front thigh is almost parallel to the floor. Press through your front heel to extend your knee and bring your leg to the starting position as you exhale. Take another breath and extend the opposite leg forward. Alternate between left and right until you’re done.

title: Overhead Plate Raise
howTo: Put your middle and ring fingers through the center hole of a weight plate and curl them against the plate. Have your thumb, index finger, and pinky pressed firmly on the other side. Grab a second weight plate in the same way with your other hand. Stand tall, extend your arms, and have the weight plates at your sides with your wrists neutral (palms facing your body). Bring your chest out, engage your abs, and breathe in. Raise the plates to your sides and overhead in a smooth arc motion with your knuckles facing one another at the top. Exhale. Slowly lower the plates to your sides in the same arc motion, breathing in on the way down. Repeat.

title: Overhead Press (Barbell)
howTo: Position the barbell at collarbone height on a rack or stand. Grab the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down), keeping your hands roughly shoulder-width apart. Tuck yourself underneath the bar, retract your shoulders, engage your abs, and take a breath. Unrack the barbell and take a couple of steps back. Have your feet hip-width apart and the barbell at upper chest level. Take another breath and press the bar in a straight vertical line, moving your head slightly back. Press until your arms are straight and exhale. Lower the bar in the same straight line until it ends up at neck level. Breathe in on the way down. Once finished, move forward and carefully rack the barbell.

title: Overhead Press (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and squeeze your glutes. Take a breath and lift the dumbbells to your sides. Bend your elbows at 90 degrees. Inhale again and press the weights up and in, tapping them lightly at the top. Breathe out. Lower the dumbbells to your sides and breathe in. Stop when your elbows are slightly lower than your shoulders and initiate the next rep.

title: Overhead Press (Smith Machine)
howTo: Position the Smith machine bar at collarbone height and add weight plates. Stand in front of the bar and grab it with an even overhand grip. Your hands should be shoulder-width apart. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Unrack the bar. Take another breath and press the bar overhead, exhaling at the top. Slowly lower the bar, breathing in on the way down.

title: Overhead Squat
howTo: Grab a barbell with a double overhand grip. Use a wider grip, but not to the point of shoulder discomfort. Raise the barbell overhead and straighten your arms. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and position your feet in a comfortable stance for squatting. Take a breath and descend. Keep your heels in contact with the floor and move down until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Pause for a moment and press through your heels to drive yourself up. Extend your knees and hips fully, exhaling near the top and keeping the barbell directly overhead.

title: Overhead Triceps Extension (Cable)
howTo: Set the pulley at mid-thigh height, attach a rope, and select the correct load. Grab both ends of the rope with an overhand grip (palms facing your body). In one motion, turn away from the machine and dip slightly to raise the rope over your shoulders, positioning it behind your head. Your elbows should be bent and to the sides of your head. With your chest out, lean forward slightly, bring one foot forward for balance, and brace your abs. Breathe in and extend your elbows, spreading the rope at the top. Exhale. Slowly bend your elbows as you breathe in.

title: Partial Glute Bridge (Barbell)
howTo: Sit down with a loaded barbell over your hips and your upper back positioned against the edge of a flat gym bench. Grab the bar evenly for support and plant your feet flat on the floor with toes pointing slightly out. Brace your abs, breathe in, and press your hips against the barbell. Push through your heels and engage your glutes to raise the bar and your hips toward the ceiling. Pause when your hips align with your upper body. Squeeze your glutes at the top and exhale. Slowly lower your buttocks several inches as you breathe in. Keep your abs tense and stop when you notice that your knees begin to move back toward your hips. Extend your hips again and exhale. Do the required number of reps in this partial range of motion and lower your buttocks to the floor.

title: Pause Squat (Barbell)
howTo: Set the barbell at collarbone height and add the appropriate amount of weight to it. Place both hands evenly on the bar and grab it. Tuck your head underneath and position your trapezius against the bar. Engage your entire body and extend your knees to unrack the bar. Take a couple of careful steps back, assume a comfortable squatting stance, take a deep breath, and engage your abs. Squat to a comfortable depth and pause for two to three seconds. Press through your heels to return to the top as you exhale.

title: Pendlay Row (Barbell)
howTo: Stand in front of a barbell with your feet in a comfortable stance and your toes pointing slightly out. Lean forward and grab the bar with a double overhand grip. Bring your shoulders back to straighten your back. Your shoulders should be slightly in front of the barbell and higher than your hips. Take a breath and row the barbell to your chest as you exhale. Lower the barbell slowly and rest it on the floor as you breathe in. Row the barbell from a dead stop.

title: Pendulum Squat (Machine)
howTo: Load the appropriate amount of weight plates. Position yourself inside the machine, stepping on the platform with your upper back against the pads. Have your feet in a comfortable squatting stance with your toes pointing slightly out. Grab the handles at the sides of your head (or before you). Retract your shoulder blades, engage your abs, and take a deep breath. Fully extend your knees to lift the weight and use the handles to unrack it. Squat slowly until your knees form a 90-degree angle, and pause briefly. Press through your heels to return to the top position as you exhale.

title: Pike Pushup
howTo: Get down on all fours. Have your knees and hands on the floor. Straighten your legs and support your lower body on your toes. Bring your buttocks back toward the ceiling to fold your body for the starting position. Keep your shoulders back to maintain a neutral spine. Take a breath and lower your body to the floor by bending your elbows. Go down until your forehead is close to the floor, and press yourself back to the top as you exhale.

title: Pinwheel Curl (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall with your shoulders back and chest out. Position both dumbbells slightly in front of your thighs with your palms facing in and back. Take a breath and curl one dumbbell in toward your chest until it comes slightly higher than your elbow. Breathe out. Lower the dumbbell to the starting position, fully extending your arm. Breathe in on the way down. Repeat with your other arm. Keep alternating.

title: Pistol Squat
howTo: Stand tall with your arms extended forward, feet flat on the floor, shoulders back, and abs engaged. Lift your left foot a few inches off the floor and take a breath. Descend into a squat by bending your right knee. Allow your left leg to travel in front of your body. Go down as low as possible––ideally, until your thigh is parallel to the floor. Press through your right heel and move back to the top as you exhale. Once finished training one side, plant your left foot on the floor, and do the same number of reps.

title: Plank
howTo: Get down on the floor. Extend your body. Support your lower body on your toes and your upper body on your forearms. Keep your shoulders neutral and abs engaged. Hold the position for as long as possible, breathing steadily as you do.

title: Plank Pushup
howTo: Get down on all fours. Extend your legs and support your lower body on your toes. Place your forearms flat on the floor with your elbows underneath your shoulders. Engage your abs and take a breath. Extend your arms one at a time by placing your hands flat on the floor and pushing yourself up, exhaling as you extend both arms. Reverse the motion by placing your forearms flat on the floor as you breathe in. Keep alternating between the two positions.

title: Plate Curl
howTo: Grab the sides of a weight plate and position it in front of your hips. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Take a breath and curl the plate until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows. Exhale at the top. Slowly extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Plate Front Raise
howTo: Grab a weight plate and position your hands on the sides with your arms straight. Bring your shoulders back, have your feet in a comfortable stance, look forward, and take a breath. Engage your abs and lift the plate in front of your body while keeping your arms straight. Breathe out. Slowly lower the plate to your hips as you breathe in.

title: Plate Press
howTo: Grab a weight plate by its sides and lift it in front of your chest. Have your feet in a comfortable stance, engage your abs, and retract your shoulder blades. Take a breath and press the plate forward, fully extending your arms and exhaling. Slowly bend your arms, bringing the plate to your chest as you breathe in.

title: Plate Squeeze (Svend Press)
howTo: Grab a weight plate and stand tall. Squeeze the plate flat between your hands and lift it to your chest. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and have your feet in a comfortable position. Take a breath and press the plate forward until your arms are straight. Exhale. Slowly bring the plate to your chest as you breathe in.

title: Power Clean
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell with your feet underneath it and shins a couple of inches away. Bend down and grab the barbell with an even, overhand grip. Bring your chest out as much as possible. Your hips should be slightly higher than your knees in this position. Initiate the first pull by deadlifting the barbell off the floor as you press your heels into the ground. Make the initial pull powerful. Once the barbell travels above your knees, keep driving your hips forward as you begin pulling the weight in a straight vertical line. The sequence should happen in one smooth motion. As the barbell travels up, squat, catch it, and have it land over your shoulders and upper chest. As the barbell lands in a front rack position, squat up to the top, straightening your knees fully. Breathe out at the top. Lower the barbell to the floor while keeping your back neutral.

title: Power Snatch
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell. Position your feet underneath the bar with your shins a couple of inches away. Lean forward and grab the bar with a double overhand grip. Use a wide grip, but not to the point of shoulder discomfort. Lower your hips to position your shins against the barbell and bring your chest out to straighten your back. Take a breath and engage your abs. Pull the bar in a straight line, keeping it close to your shins. As soon as the bar passes your knees, initiate the second, more aggressive pull and simultaneously extend your hips. With the bar traveling in a straight vertical line, engage your upper body to continue pulling the bar to chest level. As soon as the bar is high enough, bend your knees to tuck under the weight and receive it in an overhead position with your arms straight. Complete the repetition by extending your legs and exhaling. Slowly lower the barbell to the floor while keeping it close to your body.

title: Preacher Curl (Barbell)
howTo: Load the bar and adjust the seat height of the preacher bench. You should be able to place your upper arms on the pad and maintain an upright torso. Position yourself over the machine, grab the bar with an even underhand grip (palms facing the ceiling), lift the bar, and sit down. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and breathe in. Lower the bar until your arms are almost straight. Lift the bar until your forearms are almost upright, and breathe out. Extend your arms slowly as you breathe in.

title: Preacher Curl (Dumbbell)
howTo: Set the height of the preacher bench seat. You should be able to comfortably place the back of your upper arms against the pad. Grab a pair of dumbbells and sit down. Place your arms on the pad, bend your elbows, and have your wrists facing your body. Engage your abs and take a breath. Lower the dumbbells until your arms are straight. Curl the weights to the top position, stopping when your forearms are almost upright. Breathe out.

title: Preacher Curl (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Adjust the seat’s height. You should be able to put your triceps against the pad while seated. Sit down, extend your arms, and grab the handles. Engage your abs and take a breath. Bend your arms to lift the weight until your forearms are almost upright. Exhale at the top. Slowly extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Press Under
howTo: Grab a barbell with a double overhand grip. Use a wide grip, but not to the point of shoulder discomfort. Lift the barbell and position it on your upper back behind your neck. Have your feet in a comfortable stance, bring your shoulders back, and engage your abs. Inhale and simultaneously press the barbell overhead as you descend into a squat. Move down until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Press through your heels to straighten your legs and breathe out as you place the barbell on your upper back. Inhale and repeat.

title: Pull Up
howTo: Stand underneath a pull-up bar. Reach up and grab the bar with an even, overhand grip (palms facing forward). Have your hands slightly wider than shoulder level apart. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Bend your knees to lift your feet off the floor and hang from the bar. Take a breath and pull yourself up by bending your elbows. Move up as much as possible––ideally, until your chin is over the pull-up bar. Exhale. Lower yourself slowly by extending your elbows and breathe in on the way down.

title: Pull Up (Assisted)
howTo: Select the appropriate load on the pull-up machine. Grab the pull-up bar with an overhand grip (palms facing forward). Place your knees on the padded platform. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Pull yourself up until your chin is slightly over the bar and exhale. Lower yourself and fully straighten your arms as you breathe in.

title: Pull Up (Band)
howTo: Wrap a looped resistance band on a pull-up bar and let it hang. Put your feet on the band loop one at a time as you grab the pull-up bar with an overhand grip. Your hands should be slightly more than shoulder-width apart. With your body suspended, bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Pull yourself up and bring your chin over the bar as you breathe out. Pause at the top for a moment and slowly lower yourself, extending your arms fully. Breathe in as you move down.

title: Pull Up (Weighted)
howTo: Attach a weight plate to yourself with a weight belt. Reach up and grab the pull-up bar with an even overhand grip. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Pull yourself up in one fluid motion and exhale at the top. Lower yourself slowly and breathe in, keeping your feet off the floor.

title: Pullover (Dumbbell)
howTo: Pick a dumbbell and set it on the floor next to a gym bench. Position your upper back against the flat gym bench. Bend your knees and have your feet flat on the floor. Reach down and grab the dumbbell. Position the weight over your torso with your hands flat against the top weight plate. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and inhale. Lower the dumbbell behind your head while keeping your arms straight. Hold for a moment and pull the dumbbell over to the starting position as you exhale.

title: Pullover (Machine)
howTo: Add the appropriate load to the machine and adjust the seat height. Sit down and place your lower triceps and elbows on the pads, with your hands grasping the handles for additional support. Breathe in, engage your abs, and pull the weight down until your elbows are at your sides. Exhale. Raise your arms to the starting overhead position as you breathe in.

title: Push Press
howTo: Have the barbell in a front rack position: your hands outside shoulder-width level with a full grip on the bar as it sits atop your shoulders. Position your feet at roughly hip-width level with your toes pointed slightly out. Take a deep breath and initiate the dip phase by bending your knees and hips as you keep your torso upright. As you dip a few inches, change the direction by pushing through your heels to extend your knees and hips. Use your lower body’s momentum to push through your torso and lift the bar off your shoulders. Immediately push through your elbows to extend the bar overhead, exhaling at the top. Slowly lower the barbell to the initial position, resting atop your shoulders as you breathe in.

title: Push Up
howTo: Get down on all fours. Extend your body into a push-up position. Have your hands flat on the floor, fingertips rotated slightly out, and shoulder blades retracted. Have your legs straight and your toes supporting your lower body. Your ankles, knees, hips, and shoulders should be in a straight line. Take a breath and lower yourself by bending your elbows. Descend as low as possible––ideally, until your face is an inch or two from the floor. Hold the bottom position for a moment and press yourself back to the top as you exhale.

title: Push Up (Weighted)
howTo: Get down on your knees and hands. Straighten your legs and have your feet at a hip-width distance. Position your hands slightly wider than shoulder-level apart, and keep your elbows tucked. Engage your abs to ensure a neutral spine position. Have someone place a weight plate on your mid-back. Take a breath and lower yourself by bending your elbows. Go down as much as your strength allows––ideally until your torso is a couple of inches from the floor. Press yourself to the top, extending your elbows and exhaling. Take another breath and repeat.

title: Push Up - Close Grip
howTo: Get down on your hands and knees. Bring your hands close. Extend your body into a push-up position. Your shoulders, hips, knees, and ankles should be aligned. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Descend by bending your elbows. Lower yourself as much as possible. Hold the bottom position for a moment and press to bring yourself to the top as you exhale.

title: Rack Pull
howTo: Set the bar at mid-shin height. Stand in front of the bar and keep your shins an inch or two away. Lean forward and grab the bar with your hands just outside your thighs. Press your shins against the barbell and simultaneously push your knees slightly against your elbows. Bring your chest out and arch your upper back. Extend your hips as you engage your upper back and drag the barbell up against your shins and thighs. Breathe out at the top. Initiate the descent by bringing your hips back. Lower the bar in the same straight line. Inhale on the way down or before the next rep.

title: Rear Delt Reverse Fly (Cable)
howTo: Set the pulleys of a double cable machine in the highest position and attach handles. Select the appropriate weight and stand in the middle of the machine. With your left shoulder facing the pulley, grab the handle with your right hand. Walk to the pulley on your right and grab the handle with your left hand. Stand in the middle of the machine, engage your abs, and extend your arms in front of your body. Take a breath and spread your arms, squeezing your upper back muscles. Exhale. Slowly bring your arms to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Rear Delt Reverse Fly (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of light dumbbells and have your feet at a hip-width distance. Hinge at the hip to lean your torso forward and maintain a neutral spine. Extend your arms and have your wrists neutral (facing one another). Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Raise both dumbbells to the sides by engaging your posterior deltoids. Lift until your elbows align with your shoulders and hold for a moment. Breathe out. Lower the dumbbells to the starting position and breathe in.

title: Rear Delt Reverse Fly (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Adjust the seat height. The handles should be at shoulder level when you're seated. Sit down and grab the handles with your palms facing down. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Extend your arms to your sides and back as you breathe out. Bring your arms to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Rear Kick (Machine)
howTo: Add the correct load and stand inside the machine with your front facing the chest pad and handles. Position one shin against the bottom pad to create a more stable position and raise your opposite leg back with the foot flat against the footplate. Lean forward and position your stomach and chest against the pad. Grab the handles. Breathe in, engage your abs, and push the platform back and up in one smooth motion until your knee is fully extended. Exhale. Slowly bend your knee as you breathe in. Push again and exhale. Once finished training one side, switch leg positions and do the same number of reps.

title: Renegade Row (Dumbbell)
howTo: Set a pair of hexagonal dumbbells parallel to one another and at a distance where you can grab them and perform push-ups. Get down and grab both dumbbells. Extend your legs to straighten your body and bring your chest out. Your arms and body should be straight. Space your feet apart for better balance. Shift your weight slightly to the left. Take a breath and row the right dumbbell until your elbow aligns with your torso. Exhale at the top. Lower the weight to the starting position and breathe in. Lean slightly to the right and row the left dumbbell. Continue alternating until you're finished.

title: Reverse Crunch
howTo: Lie on the floor, place your arms at your sides, and have your hands flat. Bend your knees at a 90-degree angle and position them directly over your hips. Take a breath, engage your abs, and bring your knees to your upper torso in one fluid motion, lifting your buttocks off the floor. Exhale. Slowly bring your knees to the starting position and breathe in.

title: Reverse Curl (Barbell)
howTo: Grab a barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing your body). Stand tall, have your feet in a comfortable stance, and retract your shoulder blades. Engage your abs and take a breath. Curl the barbell and bend your arms until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows. Breathe out. Slowly extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Reverse Curl (Cable)
howTo: Select the appropriate load, set the pulley to the lowest position, and attach a straight bar. Lean forward, grab the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down), and stand up. Take a step back to lift the weight off its stack. Retract your shoulder blades and engage your abs. Curl the weight in one fluid motion until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows and exhale. Keep your elbows at your side. Slowly extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Reverse Curl (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Bring your arms in front of your body and have the dumbbells against your upper thighs. Your wrists should be pronated (palms facing your body). Retract your shoulder blades, engage your abs, and take a breath. Curl the dumbbells up until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows, and breathe out. Slowly extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Reverse Fly Single Arm (Cable)
howTo: Position a pulley at shoulder height and attach a handle. Select the appropriate load, grab the handle with your right hand, and take a diagonal step to the right and back to lift the weight from its stack. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and breathe in. Extend your arm to your right side and back, squeezing your shoulder muscle and breathing out. Slowly bring your arm in front of your body as you breathe in. Repeat. Once finished, grab the handle with your left hand, take two steps to the left, and do the same number of reps.

title: Reverse Grip Concentration Curl
howTo: Grab a dumbbell and sit on the edge of a flat gym bench. Spread your legs and have your feet flat on the floor. Grab the dumbbell with your right hand and rotate the wrist in, having the palm facing in. Place your right elbow against your inner thigh. Take a breath and lift the dumbbell by bending your arm. Lift until your wrist is slightly higher than your elbow and breathe out. Extend your arm as you breathe in. Once finished, do the same number of reps for your other side.

title: Reverse Grip Lat Pulldown (Cable)
howTo: Select the appropriate weight. Adjust the pad height so your legs fit snugly. Stand tall and grab the bar with an even underhand grip (palms facing back). Sit down and bring your shoulders back. Take a breath and pull the bar to your chest. Exhale immediately. Extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Reverse Hyperextension
howTo: Add the appropriate weight to the machine. Adjust the foot pads to be against your Achilles heels when you lie on top of the machine. Position yourself on the machine with your hips on the edge of the pad and grab the handles for support. Secure your feet between the bottom pads. Bring your shoulders back and take a breath. Raise your legs up in one fluid motion until your body is straight, exhaling at the top. Slowly lower your legs to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Reverse Lunge
howTo: Stand tall with your shoulders back, abs tight, and hands on your hips. Have your feet in a comfortable stance with your toes pointing slightly out. Take a breath and bring the right leg back, landing on the ball of the right foot. Sink into a lunge by bending your front leg. A good stride length is when your front shin remains relatively vertical (knee over ankle) as you descend. Descend until your front thigh is almost parallel to the floor and your rear knee is an inch or two from the floor. Pause at the bottom and engage your front leg to bring yourself back to the top. Bring the right leg to the starting position and exhale. Take another breath as you lunge back with your left leg. Keep alternating.

title: Reverse Lunge (Barbell)
howTo: Place a barbell on your back and hold it with both hands. Stand tall with your shoulders back, abs engaged, and feet in a comfortable stance. Take a breath and bring your right leg back, landing on the ball of the foot. Sink into a controlled lunge by bending your front leg. Descend until your front thigh is almost parallel to the floor and your rear knee is an inch or two from the floor. Your front shin should be relatively vertical as you move down. Pause at the bottom and push through your front heel to extend your leg, bringing yourself to the top. Exhale. Inhale again and lunge back with your left leg, landing on the ball of the foot. Keep alternating until you finish the set.

title: Reverse Lunge (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Have your feet in a comfortable stance, bring your shoulders back, and engage your abs. Take a breath and bring the right leg back, landing on the ball of the right foot. Immediately sink into a lunge by bending your front leg. Descend until your front thigh is almost parallel to the floor and your rear knee is an inch or two from the floor. A good stride length is when your front shin remains relatively vertical (knee over ankle) as you descend. Pause at the bottom and engage your front leg to bring yourself back to the top as you exhale. Take another breath and lunge back with your left leg, landing on the ball of your left foot. Keep alternating.

title: Reverse Plank
howTo: Sit on the floor with your legs straight and feet together. Extend your arms to your sides and behind your body. Keep your elbows straight, and place your palms flat on the floor. With your hands planted firmly, contract your glutes and extend your hips toward the ceiling as you simultaneously raise your torso. Have your head, neck, torso, and legs in a straight line. Keep your arms straight and look up. Keep your glutes and midsection engaged, breathe steadily, and hold the plank position. Once you’re done, drop your buttocks to the floor and take a break.

title: Ring Dips
howTo: Adjust the height of the rings to be at roughly stomach level. Stand between the rings and grab them with both hands. Keep your arms straight and elbows close to your body. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and squeeze your glutes. Take a breath and raise your feet off the floor one at a time. Once you're confident that you're stable enough, take another breath and bend your arms. Lower yourself gradually until your elbows are somewhat bent, and you feel a stretch in your chest. Press through your triceps and return to the top as you exhale. Avoid explosive movements because these can lead to instability.

title: Ring Pull Up
howTo: Secure a pair of rings and set them at pull-up bar height. Reach up and grab the rings with an overhand grip (palms facing forward). Bring your shoulders back and down and engage your abs. Bend your knees or bring your feet slightly forward to suspend yourself in the air. Breathe in and pull yourself up in one fluid motion. Exhale at the top. Slowly extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Ring Push Up
howTo: Secure a pair of rings and set them just an inch or two above the ground. Get down on your knees and grab the rings. Bring your shoulders back and flex your triceps, chest, and abs for maximum stability. Extend your legs to assume a push-up position. Support your lower body on your toes. Flex your glutes for additional stability. Breathe in and slowly lower yourself to the ground while keeping your elbows close to your torso. Descend as much as your strength allows and push back to the top as you exhale. Breathe in and repeat.

title: Romanian Deadlift (Barbell)
howTo: Grab a barbell with a double overhand grip (palms facing back). Your hands should be shoulder-width apart. Stand tall, retract your shoulder blades, engage your abs, and have your feet in a comfortable stance. Take a breath and lean forward by bringing your buttocks back (hinging at the hip). Your back should be neutral from start to finish. Lower the bar in a straight vertical line, keeping it close to your body. You should feel a stretch in your hamstrings. Straighten your body by driving your hips forward and exhale near the top.

title: Romanian Deadlift (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Position the weights in front of your hips with your palms facing your body. Bring your shoulders back, position your feet a few inches apart, and take a breath. Lean your torso forward by hinging at the hip. Your back should remain neutral, and you should feel a stretch in your posterior thigh muscles. Lower the dumbbells in a straight line until they are below knee level. Extend your hips forward and exhale.

title: Rope Cable Curl
howTo: Set the pulley in the lowest position and attach a rope. Select the appropriate weight on the cable machine. Bend forward and grab both ends of the rope with a neutral grip (palms facing one another). Stand tall, bring your shoulders back, and step back to lift the weight from its stack. Take a breath and curl the cable by bending your elbows. Lift until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows, and breathe out. Extend your arms slowly and breathe in.

title: Rope Straight Arm Pulldown
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Set the pulley to the highest position and attach a rope. Grab both ends of the rope with your hands neutral (facing one another) and straighten your arms. Take a step back, engage your abs, and retract your shoulders. Take a breath and pull the rope to your hips while keeping your arms straight. Breathe out. Slowly bring the rope to the top as you breathe in.

title: Rowing Machine
howTo: Select the appropriate resistance and sit down. Reach forward and grab the cable attachment. Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the footplates. With your arms straight, inhale and push yourself back by engaging your thigh muscles. As you move back, row the cable to your torso and exhale. Extend your arms and bend your knees simultaneously to bring yourself back to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Running
howTo: Stand tall with your shoulders back. Move forward and aim for a midfoot strike: landing the middle of your foot on the ground. Keep your arms bent and hands relaxed. Move your arms in sync with your feet in a natural way. Land lightly on your feet, but avoid bouncing up and down as you move forward. Keep your torso upright, core engaged, and gaze directed a few feet in front of you. Maintain a steady breath, aiming to inhale through the nose and exhale through the mouth.

title: Russian Twist (Bodyweight)
howTo: Sit down on the floor. Bend your knees and have your feet flat on the ground. Bring your arms in front of your torso and lean back as you engage your abs. Take a breath and twist your torso to the right, holding for a moment and exhaling. Immediately rotate your torso to the starting position as you breathe in. Twist to the left and breathe out. Keep alternating.

title: Russian Twist (Weighted)
howTo: Grab a weight plate or dumbbell and sit on the floor. Bend your knees and plant your feet flat on the ground. You can secure your feet under a bench or have a friend hold them down. Grab the weight, position it in front of your torso, and extend your arms. Lean your torso slightly back and engage your abs. Take a breath and move the weight to the right, twisting your torso in that direction as you exhale. Bring the weight to the starting position as you breathe in. Twist to the left and exhale. Keep alternating.

title: Scapular Pull Ups
howTo: Grab a pull-up bar with an even overhand grip (palms facing forward). Engage your abs and lift your feet off the floor to suspend yourself in the air. As you hang from the bar, keep your abs and glutes engaged to remain still and not swing back and forth. Take a breath and initiate the scapular pull-up by bringing your shoulders back and down. Keep your arms straight. Doing so will elevate your body slightly. Hold the retracted position for a second or two as you breathe out. Relax your body and allow your shoulders to move forward and up, breathing in as you do.

title: Seated Cable Row - Bar Grip
howTo: Select the appropriate load and sit down. Reach ahead and grab the bar evenly. Sit back and position your feet on the footplates. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Take a breath and pull the handle attachment to your upper stomach. Breathe out. Hold for a moment and extend your arms fully as you breathe in.

title: Seated Cable Row - Bar Wide Grip
howTo: Attach a curved bar to a seated cable row machine. Select the appropriate weight. Sit down, place your feet on the platform, and bend your knees slightly. Lean forward and grab the bar with a double overhand grip (palms facing down). Lean back to lift the weight from its stack, engage your abs, and retract your shoulder blades. Take a breath and row the bar to your stomach, squeezing your back and breathing out. Slowly extend your arms and breathe in.

title: Seated Cable Row - V Grip (Cable)
howTo: Attach a V-handle to a seated row machine and select the appropriate weight. Sit down and grab the handle with both hands. Place your feet on the platform and sit back. Maintain a slight bend in your knees. Retract your shoulder blades and engage your abs. Keep your torso upright, and don't lean too far back. Take a deep breath and pull the handle to your stomach in one fluid motion, pausing briefly and exhaling. Slowly extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Seated Calf Raise
howTo: Load the machine, sit down, and adjust the pad. Your thighs should fit snugly, allowing you to unrack the weight by extending your ankles. Position your thighs against the pad and grab the handles firmly. Take a breath and unrack the weight by flexing your calves. Put the safety bar to the side and flex your ankles to lower the weight until you feel a stretch in your calves. Press through the balls of your feet and flex your calves, exhaling near the top.

title: Seated Chest Flys (Cable)
howTo: Select the same weight on both stacks of a double cable machine. Set both pulleys at the same height––roughly stomach level. Attach handles to both pulleys and position an upright bench facing away and in front of the cable machine. Grab the handles one by one, walk over to the gym bench, and sit down. Extend your arms in front of your body, retract your shoulder blades, inhale, and squeeze your abs. Slowly bring your arms to your sides until you feel a stretch in your chest muscles. Bring your hands together and flex your chest as you exhale.

title: Seated Dip Machine
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Sit down and grab the handles to your sides. Plant your feet on the floor, bring your shoulders back, and engage your abs. Take a breath and press the handles down, fully extending your arms and exhaling. Bend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Seated Incline Curl (Dumbbell)
howTo: Adjust a gym bench at an incline of 65 to 75 degrees. Grab a pair of dumbbells and sit down. Have your arms to your sides with your palms facing one another. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Curl both dumbbells, rotating your wrists out, and exhale. Lift the weights until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows. Your palms should face the ceiling at the top position. Extend your arms slowly and breathe in.

title: Seated Lateral Raise (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of light dumbbells and sit at the edge of a flat gym bench. Hold a dumbbell in each hand, have your arms at your sides, and bring your palms to a neutral position (facing your body). Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Inhale and raise the dumbbells to your sides until they are at shoulder level. Breathe out. Slowly lower the weights to your sides as you breathe in.

title: Seated Leg Curl (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Adjust the pad position. It should be against your Achilles' heels as you sit down. Sit down, position the back of your lower legs against the pad, and grab the handles at your sides. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and inhale. Bend your knees by contracting your hamstrings and breathe out. Hold for a moment and slowly extend your knees as you breathe in.

title: Seated Overhead Press (Barbell)
howTo: Position an upright gym bench inside a squat rack. Place the barbell on the rack and sit on the bench. You should be able to reach and unrack the barbell while seated and with your arms slightly bent. Plant your feet on the floor. Reach up and grab the barbell with an even overhand grip (palms facing forward). Your hands should be roughly shoulder-width apart. Engage your abs, take a breath, and carefully unrack the bar. Bring the bar to your chest, lean back into the bench, and bring your chest out. Take another breath and press the bar overhead, exhaling as you reach the top. Lower the bar to collarbone level as you breathe in. Your elbows should be slightly lower than your shoulders at the bottom position. Press the barbell again. Once finished, carefully rack the bar.

title: Seated Overhead Press (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and sit on a flat gym bench or plyo box. Place the weights on your thighs, bring your shoulders back, and engage your abs. In one motion, thrust the weights and position them to your sides. Take a breath and press the dumbbells overhead, tapping them at the top as you exhale. Slowly lower the dumbbells until your elbows are slightly lower than your shoulders, breathing in on the way down.

title: Seated Palms Up Wrist Curl
howTo: Grab a pair of light dumbbells and sit on a flat gym bench. Place your forearms on your thighs with your wrists just in front of your knees. Take a breath and lower the dumbbells by extending your wrists. Immediately lift the weights by flexing your forearm muscles. Hold the top position for a moment and exhale.

title: Seated Row (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Sit down and grab the handles with a neutral grip (hands facing one another). Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Take a breath and pull the handles simultaneously. Squeeze your back as you breathe out. Extend your arms slowly and breathe in.

title: Seated Shoulder Press (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Sit down and bring your shoulder blades into the back support. Plant your feet flat on the floor. Grab the handles to your sides and keep your palms facing forward. Take a breath and engage your abs. Press the weight straight up until your arms are fully extended. Breathe out at the top. Lower the weight to the starting position and breathe in.

title: Seated Triceps Press
howTo: Set the appropriate weight and sit down. Grab the handles at your sides with a comfortable grip. Bring your shoulders back, take a breath, and squeeze your abs. Push the handles down until your elbows lock out and breathe out. Slowly bend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Seated Wrist Extension (Barbell)
howTo: Grab a loaded bar with an overhand grip (palms facing your body). Sit on the edge of a flat gym bench and plant your feet on the floor. Position your forearms on top of your thighs with your wrists in front of your knees. Take a breath and lower the barbell in front of your knees by flexing your wrists. Lift the bar by extending your wrists and exhale.

title: Shoulder Press (Dumbbell)
howTo: Set an adjustable gym bench at a close to 90-degree angle (almost upright back support). Grab a pair of dumbbells and sit down. Place the weights on top of your thighs. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Lift the dumbbells and kick them up with your thighs. Position the weights to your sides. Take another breath and press the dumbbells up and in, tapping them at the top as you exhale.
