Lower the dumbbells until your elbows are slightly lower than your shoulders, breathing in on the way down.

title: Shoulder Press (Machine Plates)
howTo: Add the appropriate amount of weight to the machine, ensuring it’s equal on both sides. Adjust the seat height. Your elbows should be slightly lower than your shoulders as you grab the handles when you sit down. Sit down and grab the handles. Lean back and retract your shoulder blades. Take a deep breath and engage your abs. Press the weight up in one fluid motion and exhale at the top. Slowly lower the weight as you breathe in.

title: Shoulder Taps
howTo: Get down on your hands and knees. Extend your legs and support your lower body on your toes. Plant your hands flat on the floor at roughly shoulder-width distance. Engage your abs to straighten your body and retract your shoulder blades. Inhale and descend by bending your elbows. Go down as much as possible and press through your palms to move to the top. Immediately lift your left hand off the floor and tap your right shoulder as you exhale. Bring the hand to the floor and do another push-up as you breathe in on the way down. Lift your right hand to tap your left shoulder. Keep alternating.

title: Shrug (Barbell)
howTo: Grab a barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing back). Your hands should be just outside your thighs. Straighten your arms, engage your abs, and retract your shoulder blades. Take a breath and shrug the barbell by elevating your shoulders, exhaling at the top. Imagine that you’re trying to touch your ears with your trapezius. Lower your shoulder blades as you breathe in.

title: Shrug (Cable)
howTo: Set the pulley to the lowest position and attach a straight bar. Select the appropriate weight. Lean forward and grab the straight bar with an overhand grip (palms facing your body). Stand up and step back to lift the weight off its stack. Bring your shoulders back, squeeze your glutes, and engage your abs. Take a breath and shrug, lifting your shoulder blades. Exhale at the top. Slowly lower your shoulder blades as you breathe in.

title: Shrug (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells, stand tall, position your arms to your sides, and have your wrists neutral. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Take a breath, shrug your shoulders as high as possible, and squeeze your trapezius muscle at the top. Keep your arms straight. Exhale at the top and lower your shoulder blades to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Shrug (Machine)
howTo: Add the appropriate load to the machine. Stand between the handles, grab them, and stand tall. With your arms straight and at your sides, take a deep breath and engage your abs. Shrug your shoulders up in one fluid motion, exhaling at the top. Don’t bend your elbows. Lower your shoulder blades as you breathe in.

title: Shrug (Smith Machine)
howTo: Position the Smith machine bar at hip height and add weight. Face away from the bar and grab it with an overhand grip (palms facing back). Engage your abs and take a breath. Rotate the bar to unrack it. Shrug your shoulders as high as possible and squeeze your traps at the top as you breathe out. Lower your shoulder blades and breathe in. Once finished, rotate the bar to rack it on the Smith machine.

title: Side Bend
howTo: Stand sideways on a back hyperextension machine. Your right upper thigh should be against the pad, and your feet should be secure. Engage your abs and place your hands behind your head. Take a breath and bend your torso to the right. Squeeze your obliques to bend your torso to the left and exhale. Once finished, rotate your body 180 degrees and do the same number of reps.

title: Side Bend (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a dumbbell with your right hand and stand tall with your arm to your side. Place your left hand behind your head. Engage your abs and bring your shoulders back. Take a breath and crunch your torso to the left. Exhale. Extend your torso and breathe in. Crunch again. Once finished, grab the dumbbell with the opposite hand and do the same number of reps.

title: Side Plank
howTo: Lie on your left side. Place your left forearm on the floor for balance. Stack your legs and bring one foot over the other. Take a breath and engage your midsection to lift your hips off the floor. Hold the position for 30 to 60 seconds and relax. Turn to your other side and hold the same position.

title: Single Arm Cable Crossover
howTo: Set the pulley at chest height and attach a handle. Select the appropriate load. Grab the handle, step away from the machine, and turn your side to it. The cable pulley should be to your side and slightly behind your body. Bring your shoulders back, breathe in, and engage your abs. With your arm slightly bent and to the side, bring the cable in front of your torso. Squeeze your chest and exhale. Slowly bring your arm to the side and breathe in. Once finished, grab the handle with your other hand, have the pulley at your side and slightly behind your body, and do the same number of reps.

title: Single Arm Cable Row
howTo: Set a cable pulley in a low position or at chest level and attach a handle. Select the appropriate load, grab the handle, and step back to lift the weight off its stack. Bring your shoulders back, stagger your stance, bend your knees slightly, engage your abs, and breathe in. Pull the handle to your side, aligning your elbow to your torso. Squeeze your back and hold the position for a moment and exhale. Slowly extend your arm and breathe in. Once you have finished training one side, grab the handle with your opposite hand and repeat for the same number of reps.

title: Single Arm Curl (Cable)
howTo: Select the appropriate weight, set the pulley to the lowest position, and attach a handle. Grab the handle with your right hand and position the palm up. Take a step back, inhale, and squeeze your abs. Curl the weight and squeeze your bicep at the top momentarily as you exhale. Slowly extend your arm as you breathe in. Once finished, grab the handle with your left hand and repeat.

title: Single Arm Landmine Press (Barbell)
howTo: Anchor one end of a barbell on a landmine attachment. Add a weight plate on the opposite barbell sleeve. Lean forward and grab the loaded side with both hands. Carefully lift the bar to chest level and stand tall. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and raise your left arm to your side. Take a breath and press the bar forward and up until your arm is straight. Exhale at the top. Slowly lower the bar to your upper chest as you breathe in. Once finished training one side, grab the bar with your left hand and do the same number of reps.

title: Single Arm Lat Pulldown
howTo: Select the appropriate weight and attach a handle to the pulley. Adjust the knee pad to a height where your thighs fit snugly underneath. Stand tall, reach up, and grab the handle with one hand. Sit down and secure your thighs underneath the pad. With your arm straight, retract your shoulder blades, engage your midsection, and inhale. Pull the cable down and in until you feel a contraction on your back. Keep your elbow in line with your torso and close to your body. Hold for a moment as you exhale. Slowly extend your arm as you breathe in before the next rep. Once finished, stand tall, grab the handle with your other hand, and do the same number of reps.

title: Single Arm Lateral Raise (Cable)
howTo: Select the appropriate load, set the pulley to the lowest position, and attach a handle. Stand with your left side facing the cable attachment and grab the handle with your right hand. Take a lateral step away to lift the weight off its stack and maintain a slight bend in your right arm. Breathe in, engage your abs, and lift your arm to the side until the wrist and elbow align with your shoulder. Exhale at the top. Slowly lower your arm as you breathe in. Once finished training one side, turn your body 180 degrees, grab the handle with your left hand, and repeat.

title: Single Arm Tricep Extension (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a dumbbell and sit on a flat gym bench or chair. Lift the weight overhead and straighten your arm. Bring your shoulders back, engage your midsection, and inhale. Lower the dumbbell behind your head while keeping your elbow steady. Bend your arm until you feel a stretch in your tricep, and extend your elbow as you exhale. Once finished, grab the weight with your other hand, and do the same number of reps.

title: Single Arm Triceps Pushdown (Cable)
howTo: Select the appropriate load, set the pulley to the highest position, and attach a rope. Grab the rope with your right hand, position your elbow to your side, and step back. Grab the cable machine's pillar with your left hand, take a deep breath, and engage your abs. Slowly extend your arm and flex your triceps as you exhale. Bend your arm as you breathe in. Once finished, grab the rope with your left hand and repeat.

title: Single Leg Extensions
howTo: Select the appropriate load on a leg extension machine. Set the pad to be just over your feet as you sit down. Adjust the back support to have your knees just in front of the edge of the seat. Sit down, position one leg against the pad, grab the handles to your sides, bring your shoulders back, and inhale. Extend your leg fully and exhale. Bend the leg as you inhale. Once finished training one side, position your opposite shin against the pad, and do the same number of reps.

title: Single Leg Glute Bridge
howTo: Place an exercise mat and lie down with your arms to your sides and hands flat on the floor. Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the floor. Raise one leg in the air. Take a breath and press through the heel of your planted foot, engaging your posterior chain to drive your hips toward the ceiling. Extend your hips until they align with your knee and shoulders, and exhale at the top. Lower your buttocks to the floor, breathing in on the way down. Switch feet position and do the same number of reps for your opposite side.

title: Single Leg Hip Thrust
howTo: Sit on the floor with your back against a flat gym bench. Bring your arms to your sides and place them flat on the gym bench. Bend your knees and have your feet flat on the floor. Lift one foot off the floor, take a breath, and engage your abs. Press through your other foot and extend your hips until they align with your torso. Breathe out. Lower your buttocks to the floor as you breathe in. Once finished, plant the opposite foot on the floor and do the same number of reps.

title: Single Leg Hip Thrust (Dumbbell)
howTo: Place a dumbbell next to a flat gym bench. Sit on the floor, position your upper back against the bench's edge, grab the weight, and place it over your hips. Bend your right knee and plant the foot on the floor. Keep your left leg straight. Take a deep breath, engage your abs, and push through your right heel to drive your hips toward the ceiling. Hold the dumbbell with both hands for safety. Pause briefly at the top as you exhale. Lower your buttocks to the floor as you breathe in. Repeat. Once finished training your right side, plant your left foot on the floor and extend your right leg. Repeat for the same number of reps.

title: Single Leg Press (Machine)
howTo: Set the appropriate load on the machine and sit down. Place one foot flat on the platform and grab the handles to your sides. Keep your other foot on the floor. Engage your abs and take a breath. Extend your leg and remove the safety. Slowly bend your knee, bringing yourself close to the platform as you breathe in. Press through your heel and extend your leg as you exhale. Once finished training one leg, place the opposite foot on the platform and do the same number of reps.

title: Single Leg Romanian Deadlift (Barbell)
howTo: Grab a barbell with a double overhand grip (palms facing back). Your hands should be shoulder-width apart. Stand tall with your feet close, back straight, shoulders back, and abs engaged. Take a breath and lean forward by hinging at the hip. Simultaneously raise your right foot off the floor, bringing the right leg back for balance. Lean forward until you feel a stretch in your left hamstring. Lower the barbell in a straight line, keeping it close to your body. Straighten your body and bring your right leg forward as you exhale. Take another breath and repeat. Once finished training one side, do the same number of reps by raising your left foot and extending the leg back.

title: Single Leg Romanian Deadlift (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a dumbbell with your right hand and position it in front of your hip (upper thigh). Raise your right foot off the floor and support yourself on the left. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and inhale. Slowly lean your torso forward by hinging at the hips. Allow your right leg to move behind your body for balance. Lean forward until you feel a stretch at the back of your left thigh. Straighten your body, squeeze your glutes, and exhale. Once finished, grab the weight with your left hand, plant your right foot on the floor, lift your left one in the air, and do the same number of reps.

title: Single Leg Standing Calf Raise
howTo: Place the ball of your right foot on an elevated platform and grab onto something for balance. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and straighten your right leg. Take a breath and lower your heel to the floor by flexing your ankle. Move down until you feel a stretch in your calve and press through your heel to extend your ankle. Squeeze your calf at the top and exhale. Once finished, place the ball of your opposite foot on the platform and do the same number of reps.

title: Single Leg Standing Calf Raise (Barbell)
howTo: Place a loaded barbell on your back, similar to how you would for the squat. Position the ball of one foot on an elevated platform. Keep your other foot on the platform for balance. Bring your shoulders back, flex your abs, and take a breath. Extend your ankle, squeezing your calve muscle at the top. Breathe out. Flex your ankle to lower yourself to the starting position as you breathe in. Once finished, position the ball of your other foot on the platform and repeat for the same number of reps.

title: Single Leg Standing Calf Raise (Dumbbell)
howTo: Hold a dumbbell in your left hand and place the ball of your right foot on an elevated platform. Keep your left foot in the air. Grab onto something for balance with your right hand. Engage your abs, take a breath, and straighten your right leg. Press through the ball of your foot to elevate yourself and squeeze your calf as you breathe out. Slowly lower your body by flexing your ankle and breathe in. You should feel a stretch in your calves. Repeat. Once finished, place the ball of your left foot on the platform and do the same number of reps.

title: Single Leg Standing Calf Raise (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load on the machine. Place the ball of your right foot on the platform and position your shoulders against the pads. Keep your leg straight. Grab the handles for support, engage your abs, and take a breath. Push through the ball of your foot to extend your ankle and squeeze your calve muscle as you breathe out. Slowly lower your ankle to the floor, stretching your calf. Breathe in on the way down. Once finished training your right calve, place your left foot on the platform and repeat for the same number of reps.

title: Sissy Squat (Weighted)
howTo: Grab a weight plate, lift it in front of your chest, and hold it with one arm. Stand before a gym bench or another object you can hold onto for balance. Grab the object for support. Have your feet a few inches apart, engage your abs, and take a breath. Descend into a squat while simultaneously leaning your torso back. As you move down, allow your heels to lift off the floor and support yourself on the balls of your feet. Go down as much as possible, feeling your quadriceps stretch. Engage your quadriceps to straighten your legs and stand tall. Exhale near the top.

title: Sit Up
howTo: Place an exercise mat on the floor and lie on it. Bend your knees and plant your feet flat on the floor. Position your hands in front of your face or behind your head. Take a breath and crunch your torso by engaging your abs, lifting your upper body to an upright position. Breathe out at the top position and slowly lower your torso as you breathe in.

title: Sit Up (Weighted)
howTo: Place a dumbbell or weight plate on the floor and lie next to it. Bend your knees and plant your feet flat on the floor. Grab the weight and position it over your chest. Take a breath and crunch your torso by engaging your abs, lifting your torso to an upright position. Exhale. Hold for a moment and lower your torso slowly as you breathe in.

title: Skullcrusher (Barbell)
howTo: Load a straight bar, lift it off the floor, and support it in front of your chest. Carefully sit on a flat gym bench and lie back while keeping the bar close to your torso. Extend your arms and bring your shoulders back. Plant your feet on the floor. Take a breath and lower the barbell to your forehead or behind your head. Pause for a moment and extend your arms while keeping your elbows in position. Exhale near the top.

title: Skullcrusher (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and lie on a flat gym bench. Straighten your arms and position the dumbbells over your chest with your wrists neutral (hands facing one another). Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and plant your feet flat on the floor. Take a breath and slowly bend your elbows to bring the dumbbells to the sides of your head. Hold the position momentarily and extend your arms by squeezing your triceps. Exhale near the top.

title: Sled Push
howTo: Load a sled with the appropriate amount of weight. Lean forward, grab the two handles, and straighten your arms. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and have the balls of your feet on the ground. Take a breath and press through the balls of your feet to move forward. Move forward slowly, pressing through your feet. Breathe steadily and go for as long as possible.

title: Snatch
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell. Your feet should be underneath the bar, and your shins should be a couple of inches away. Lean forward and grab the bar with a wide snatch grip. Lower your hips, bring your shoulders back to straighten your back, and adjust yourself to the bar. Your knees should extend over the bar. Engage your abs and take a breath. Powerfully drive through your heels as if trying to squat the weight. As the barbell glides close to your body and reaches your hips, press through your heels for a second time, powerfully extending your knees and hips. Simultaneously, pull the bar up in a vertical line. Drop by bending your knees and receive the barbell overhead with your arms straight. Press through your heels to perform an overhead squat and exhale at the top. Lower the barbell to the floor, keeping it close to your body.

title: Spider Curl (Barbell)
howTo: Set the incline of a gym bench at roughly 45 degrees. Position yourself on the bench with your stomach and chest flat against the back support. Reach down and grab the barbell with an even underhand grip (palms facing forward). Alternatively, have someone hand you the bar. Engage your abs and take a breath. Curl the barbell while keeping your elbows stationary. Bend your arms until your wrists are higher than your elbows. Squeeze your biceps and breathe out. Slowly lower the barbell and fully extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Spider Curl (Dumbbell)
howTo: Set a gym bench at a 45 to 60-degree incline. Grab a pair of dumbbells and sit on the bench with your chest and stomach against the back support. Your arms should hang down with your palms facing forward. Take a deep breath and curl both dumbbells, not moving your elbows forward. Exhale at the top. Extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Spiderman
howTo: Get down on all fours and extend your body in an elbow plank position. Have your forearms flat on the floor, supporting your upper body, and your legs extended with your toes supporting your lower body. Keep your buttocks slightly elevated, back neutral, and abs engaged for support. Take a breath, tuck your right knee to your right elbow, and exhale. Bring your knee back to the starting position as you breathe in. Tuck your left knee to your left elbow. Continue alternating between left and right until you finish the set.

title: Spinning
howTo: Adjust the seat height and get on top of the cardio machine. Select the appropriate program and grab the handles. Place your feet flat on the pedals, bring your shoulders back, and direct your gaze forward. Cycle steadily and maintain a steady breath.

title: Split Jerk
howTo: Hold a barbell at neck level with your arms wide. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and have your feet in a comfortable stance. Take a breath and descend a few inches by bending your knees. Push through your heels and immediately thrust the barbell overhead. Immediately split your stance, bringing your dominant leg forward and the non-dominant one back to receive the barbell. Position your feet in line and slowly lower the barbell to neck level as you exhale.

title: Split Squat (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Retract your shoulder blades, engage your abs, and bring your right leg forward, planting the foot flat on the ground. Take a breath and bend your right knee to descend into a split squat. Descend until your back knee taps the floor and press through your front heel to straighten your right leg. Exhale near the top. Once finished training your right leg, bring your left leg forward and do the same number of reps.

title: Squat (Band)
howTo: Take a large looped resistance band and step over it with both feet. Have your feet in a comfortable stance for squatting. Grab the opposite side of the band, raise it overhead, and place it behind your neck. Stand tall, bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and breathe in. Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Hold for a moment and press through your heels to bring yourself to the top. Exhale.

title: Squat (Barbell)
howTo: Position the barbell at collarbone height. Grab the bar with an even overhand grip (palms facing down). Tuck yourself underneath the bar and place it on top of your trapezius (upper back). Align your feet, engage your abs, take a breath, and unrack the bar by straightening your legs. Take a couple of careful steps back and bring your feet shoulder-width apart. Bring your shoulders back, inhale, and squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Keep your heels in contact with the floor. Hold for a moment and press through your heels to bring yourself back to the top. Exhale near the top. Once finished, move forward and rack the barbell carefully before relaxing your body.

title: Squat (Bodyweight)
howTo: Stand tall with your feet in a comfortable stance, shoulders back, and gaze directed forward. Lift your arms in front of you for balance and take a breath. Squat and keep your heels in contact with the floor. Move down until your thighs are parallel to the ground. Push through your heels to bring yourself to the top and exhale.

title: Squat (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall with your arms straight and to your sides. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and have your feet in a comfortable stance. Take a breath and descend by bending your knees. Move down until your thighs are parallel to the floor, and keep your heels in contact with the ground. Press through your heels to move up and exhale near the top.

title: Squat (Machine)
howTo: Position your back and shoulders against the pad. Have your feet hip-width apart with your toes pointing slightly out. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Pull the safety lever away to unrack the weight. Bend your knees to descend into a squat and keep your heels in contact with the floor. Squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Press through your heels to bring yourself to the top and exhale. Once finished, bring the safety lever back and rack the weight.

title: Squat (Smith Machine)
howTo: Position the Smith machine bar at just below collarbone height. Grab the bar with an overhand grip, tuck your head underneath, and position your upper back against the bar. Walk your feet out a bit and have them hip-width apart. Extend your knees to unrack the bar. Engage your abs, breathe in, and squat by bending your knees. Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Press through your heels to move back to the top and exhale. Once finished, rack the bar and relax your body.

title: Squat (Suspension)
howTo: Grab the handles of a suspension kit and bend your arms at a 90-degree angle. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and have your feet in a comfortable squatting stance. Inhale and squat while keeping your heels in contact with the floor. Continue until your thighs become parallel to the floor, and pause for a moment. Press through your heels and bring yourself to the top as you exhale.

title: Squat Row
howTo: Select the appropriate load on a cable machine. Set the pulley at hip height and attach a rope or handle. Grab the cable attachment, take a step back to lift the weight off its stack, and bring your shoulders back. With your arms straight, descend into a squat. Go down until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Press through your heels to move up and immediately row the weight to your stomach as you breathe out.

title: Stair Machine (Floors)
howTo: Climb on top of the machine and turn it on. Select a low speed and grab the handrails for support. Climb the moving stairs while keeping your shoulders back and abs engaged. Maintain a steady breath. Increase the speed to a sustainable pace once you’re familiar with the machine.

title: Stair Machine (Steps)
howTo: Climb on top of the machine and turn it on. Select a low speed and grab the handrails for support. Climb the moving stairs while keeping your shoulders back and abs engaged. Maintain a steady breath. Increase the speed to a sustainable pace once you’re familiar with the machine.

title: Standing Cable Glute Kickbacks
howTo: Set the pulley to the lowest position and select the appropriate weight. Attach an ankle strap to the pulley. Face the cable machine and wrap the strap around your right ankle, just above the foot. Bend forward, grab the cable machine’s pillars for balance, and engage your abs. Take a breath and bring your right leg back in one fluid motion, squeezing your glute at the top and exhaling. Slowly bring the leg to the starting position and breathe in. Once finished, wrap the strap over your other ankle, lean forward, and do the same number of reps.

title: Standing Calf Raise
howTo: Stand tall with your feet together, knees extended, shoulders back, and arms to your sides. Take a breath. Press through the balls of your feet to extend your ankles, raising yourself a couple of inches. Exhale. Lower yourself to the starting position as you breathe in.

title: Standing Calf Raise (Barbell)
howTo: Position a barbell on your upper back muscles behind your neck and hold it with both hands. Place the balls of your feet on an elevated platform and have your heels on the floor. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Take a breath and extend your ankles, lifting your heels and squeezing your calves as you exhale. Lower your heels without resting them on the floor as you breathe in.

title: Standing Calf Raise (Dumbbell)
howTo: Hold a dumbbell in one hand. Place the balls of your feet on an elevated platform. Use your free hand to grab onto something for balance. Extend your knees, squeeze your glutes, engage your abs, and retract your shoulders. Take a breath and flex your ankles, bringing your heels to the floor. Go down as much as possible, feeling your calves stretch. Hold for a moment and extend your ankles to raise yourself, squeezing your calves at the top as you exhale.

title: Standing Calf Raise (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Put your shoulders and upper back against the machine’s pad. Position the balls of your feet on the platform and extend your knees. Grab the handles by the machine’s pad for extra support. Take a breath and extend your ankles, squeezing your calves at the top, and exhale. Lower yourself slowly, feeling your calves stretch at the bottom. Breathe in on the way down.

title: Standing Calf Raise (Smith)
howTo: Set the bar at the appropriate height, where you can unrack it as you fully extend your knees. Add the appropriate weight and position a calf raise block under the bar. Position your trapezius against the bar, grab it with both hands, and place the balls of your feet on the platform. Engage your entire body and straighten your knees to unrack the bar. Inhale and slowly lower yourself by allowing your ankles to flex. Keep your knees extended. Pause briefly at the bottom and push through the balls of your feet to extend your ankles. Squeeze your calves and pause briefly as you exhale.

title: Standing Leg Curls
howTo: Select the appropriate weight on the machine. Stand tall, grab the handles for support, and position your right Achilles heel against the machine’s pad. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and take a breath. Slowly bend your leg until your ankle is slightly higher than your knee. Squeeze your hamstring muscle at the top and exhale. Slowly extend your leg as you breathe in. Once finished training one side, position your opposite Achilles heel against the pad and do the same number of reps.

title: Standing Military Press (Barbell)
howTo: Position the barbell at collarbone height on a rack or stand. Stand a few inches from the bar and grab it with an overhand grip. Have your hands shoulder-width apart. With your hands gripping the bar, tuck yourself underneath and retract your shoulders. Take a breath, engage your glutes, and push through your legs to unrack the bar. With your elbows tucked in, back straight, and gaze forward, take a couple of steps back and have your feet at hip-width distance. Take a deep breath, engage your abs, and push through your elbows in a vertical line. Push until your elbows lock out and exhale. Slowly lower the bar to neck level as you breathe in.

title: Step Up
howTo: Stand in front of a flat gym bench or plyometric box. Have your feet hip-width apart with your toes pointing slightly out. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Take a breath, lift your right leg, and plant the foot on the elevated platform. Push through the heel to extend your body and bring yourself on top of the elevated object. Exhale. Step off the platform, take another breath and extend your opposite leg.

title: Sternum Pull up (Gironda)
howTo: Reach up and grab the pull-up bar with an even overhand grip (palms facing forward). Have your hands shoulder-width apart or slightly wider. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and squeeze your glutes. Bring your shoulder blades as far back as your strength and mobility allow. Keep your midsection engaged and lean your torso back. Take a breath and pull yourself up until your lower chest touches the bar, then exhale. Lower yourself with control and breathe in as you descend.

title: Straight Arm Lat Pulldown (Cable)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Set the pulley to the highest position and attach a straight bar. Grab the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down). Step back to raise the weight from its stack. Engage your abs, retract your shoulder blades, and squeeze your glutes. You can stagger your stance (bring one foot forward and the other back) for balance. Take a breath and bring the bar to your hips in one fluid motion. Keep your arms straight and exhale. Slowly let the bar travel forward and up as you breathe in.

title: Straight Leg Deadlift
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell. Position your feet underneath and have your shins a couple of inches from the bar. Lean forward by hinging at the hips. Grab the bar with a double overhand grip (palms facing back). Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Keep your legs straight. Take a breath and pull the bar in a straight vertical line while pushing through your heels. Squeeze your glutes at the top and exhale. Slowly lower the bar in the same straight line and breathe in on the way down or before initiating the next rep.

title: Sumo Deadlift
howTo: Stand before a loaded barbell with your feet spread wide and your toes pointing out. Your shins should be close to the bar but not in contact. Bend forward and grab the barbell with a double overhand grip (palms facing back). Your hands should be shoulder-width apart, with your elbows a few inches from your inner thighs. Bring your shoulders back and lift your chest. Doing so will straighten your back, position your hips at the correct height, and bring your shins against the barbell. With your body tense and arms straight, breathe into your belly. Pull the barbell forcefully as you dig your heels into the floor. Extend your knees and hips as the barbell travels in a straight vertical line and close to your body. Squeeze your glutes at the top to complete the repetition and exhale, but don’t hyperextend your back. Lower the barbell in the same vertical line. Breathe in on the way down or before initiating the next rep.

title: Sumo Squat
howTo: Stand tall and assume a wider-than-shoulder-level stance with your toes pointing out. Retract your shoulder blades and raise your arms in front of your body for balance. Take a deep breath, engage your abs, and squat. Move down until your thighs are parallel to the floor, and press through your heels to return to the top as you exhale.

title: Sumo Squat (Barbell)
howTo: Set the barbell at collarbone height. Place your hands evenly over the bar, slightly wider than shoulder level apart. Tuck your head under the bar, placing it on your upper back. Plant your feet on the floor, take a breath, and unrack the bar. Take a couple of steps back. Widen your stance as much as possible with your toes pointing out. Bring your chest out and take a breath. Squat slowly, keeping your heels in contact with the floor. Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor, hold for a moment, and press through your heels, exhaling near the top.

title: Sumo Squat (Dumbbell)
howTo: Place a dumbbell in a vertical position on the floor and stand before it. Squat down and grab the top weight plate with both hands. Stand tall and assume a comfortable squatting stance. A wide (sumo) stand would give you more room to squat comfortably. Take a deep breath and engage your abs. Slowly descend into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Push through your heels to move back to the top as you exhale.

title: Sumo Squat (Kettlebell)
howTo: Grab a kettlebell by the handle and stand tall with your arms straight and the weight in front of your body. Assume a wider-than-shoulder-level stance, with your toes pointing out. Retract your shoulder blades, take a deep breath, and engage your abs. Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor, and pause for a moment. Press through your heels to move back to the top and exhale.

title: Superman
howTo: Lie face down on the floor with your legs straight and arms extended to the sides of your head. Have your toes, knees, thighs, stomach, and chest in contact with the floor. Take a breath and extend your arms and legs toward the ceiling as high as possible. Breathe out. Hold the arched position for a couple of seconds, and relax your body as you breathe in.

title: T Bar Row
howTo: Add some weight to the T-bar station. Step on the platform, lean forward and grab the handles with an overhand grip. Retract your shoulder blades, engage your abs, and inhale. With your back neutral, push through your heels and extend your hips a bit to lift the weight off the floor. Keep your torso almost parallel with the floor and have your arms straight. Inhale again and pull the weight until the handles get close to your torso. Breathe out at the top. Extend your arms as you breathe in. Don’t set the weight on the floor.

title: Thruster (Barbell)
howTo: Have the barbell in a front rack position: resting on your shoulders with your elbows bent and fingertips supporting. Position your feet in a comfortable squatting stance. Engage your abs and take a breath. Descend into a squat and keep your heels in contact with the floor. Move down until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Press through your heels to move up. As your legs straighten, immediately transition into an overhead press, thrusting the barbell overhead and breathing out. Lower the barbell to the front rack position as you breathe in. Descend into another squat.

title: Thruster (Kettlebell)
howTo: Grab a pair of kettlebells, stand tall, and lift them to shoulder level. Keep your elbow bent and the kettlebells on the outer side, resting against your forearms and biceps (rack position). Have your feet at a hip-width distance with your toes pointing slightly out. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, take a breath, and squat. Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor and move up, pushing through your heels. As you reach the top of the squat, press the kettlebells overhead, extending your elbows fully and exhaling. Lower the kettlebells to the sides of your shoulder, take another breath, and squat.

title: Toe Touch
howTo: Lie on the floor, straighten your legs, and raise them toward the ceiling. Engage your abs and raise your arms over your chest with your fingers pointing up. Take a breath and crunch your abs to lift your shoulder blades several inches off the floor. Touch your toes with your fingers (or crunch as high as possible in one fluid motion) and exhale. Lower your upper back to the floor as you breathe in.

title: Toes to Bar
howTo: Reach up and grab a pull-up bar with an overhand grip (palms facing forward). Bring your feet together and lift them off the floor to suspend yourself in the air. Retract your shoulder blades and engage your abs. Take a breath and lift your legs in one fluid motion. Go up as high as possible––ideally, until your toes are close to the pull-up bar you’re holding. Breathe out at the top. Slowly lower your legs to the starting position as you breathe in. Do repetitions slowly and keep your abs engaged to prevent your body from swinging back and forth.

title: Torso Rotation
howTo: Select the appropriate weight. Adjust the seat height so that you can comfortably place your forearms on the pads at your sides and grab the handles. With your knees bent, position your shins against the bottom pads and push forward to create whole-body rigidity. Breathe in and twist your lower body to the right as you exhale. It should be a fluid motion. Slowly return your lower body to the starting position as you breathe in. Rotate your legs to the left and breathe out again. Continue alternating from left to right, breathing steadily as you do reps.

title: Treadmill
howTo: Stand on the treadmill and clip the safety key to your tank top or T-shirt. Start the treadmill and gradually increase the speed from slow walking to a jog. Maintain an upright posture, direct your gaze forward, and maintain a steady breath. Keep your arms bent and move them in sync with your strides.

title: Triceps Dip
howTo: Grab a pair of parallel bars and suspend yourself in the air. Maintain an upright body, bring your shoulders back, and engage your abs. Take a breath and lower yourself by bending your elbows. Move down until your elbows are at a 90-degree angle. Hold the position for a moment and press yourself back to the top as you exhale.

title: Triceps Dip (Assisted)
howTo: Select the appropriate load on the machine. Grab the parallel bars, place your knees on the platform, and bring your legs together. Retract your shoulder blades, straighten your arms, and engage your abs. Take a breath and lower yourself by bending your arms. Descend until your elbows are at a 90-degree angle, hold for a moment, and press back to the top as you breathe out.

title: Triceps Dip (Weighted)
howTo: Attach a weight plate to yourself via a special belt. Grab a pair of parallel bars and suspend yourself in the air. Maintain an upright body, bring your shoulders back, and engage your abs. Take a breath and lower yourself by bending your elbows. Move down until your elbows are at a 90-degree angle. Hold the position for a moment and press yourself back to the top as you exhale.

title: Triceps Extension (Barbell)
howTo: Load a barbell. Grab the bar with an even overhand grip (palms facing back). Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Lift the bar to chest level. Sit down on a flat gym bench. Raise the barbell overhead and extend your arms. Take a breath and lower the bar behind your head, feeling your triceps stretch. Hold the bottom for a moment and extend your arms as you exhale.

title: Triceps Extension (Cable)
howTo: Set a cable pulley in a low position and attach a handle. Select a light load with a pin. Reach down and grab the handle with one hand. Turn your body away from the cable machine and extend your arm toward the ceiling. Place your opposite hand on your elbow for support, retract your shoulder blades, and engage your abs. Take a breath and slowly bend your elbow until you feel a stretch in your tricep. Flex your tricep to extend your arm and exhale near the top. Once finished training one tricep, grab the handle with your opposite hand and do the same number of reps.

title: Triceps Extension (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a dumbbell and stand tall. Lift the weight overhead and place your hands flat against the top weight plate with your palms facing the ceiling. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Take a breath and lower the dumbbell behind your head while keeping your elbows to the sides of your head. Bend your arms until you feel a stretch, and extend your elbows as you breathe out.

title: Triceps Extension (Machine)
howTo: Set the appropriate load and sit down. Grab the handles to your sides, bring your shoulders back into the pad, and plant your feet on the floor. Take a breath and push the handles down by extending your elbows. Straighten your arms fully and hold the position momentarily as you exhale. Bend your arms slowly and breathe in.

title: Triceps Extension (Suspension)
howTo: Grab the handles of the suspension kit and turn away from the anchor point. Extend your arms in front of your body, bring your feet together, and flex your abs. Retract your shoulders and take a breath. Lean your body forward by bending your elbows. Descend until you feel a stretch in your triceps. Flex your arm muscles to extend your body and exhale.

title: Triceps Kickback (Cable)
howTo: Set the cable pulley in a low position and select the appropriate load. Don’t add an attachment. Grab the plastic piece at the rope’s end with your right hand and take a step back. Lean your torso forward and keep your back straight. Raise your elbow to the side of your torso and inhale. Extend your arm fully and pause briefly as you exhale. Slowly bend your arm as you breathe in. Repeat. Once finished, grab the rope with your left hand and repeat for the same number of reps.

title: Triceps Kickback (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a light dumbbell and hold it to your side with your palm facing your thigh. Lean your torso forward as you push your butt back to maintain a neutral back. Lift your elbow to your side, inhale, and extend your arm by engaging your tricep. Breathe out. Hold the contraction for a second and slowly bring the dumbbell back down as you breathe in. Keep your shoulder and elbow in a stationary position throughout each repetition.

title: Triceps Pressdown
howTo: Set the appropriate weight on a cable machine. Put the pulley in the highest position and attach a straight or V-shaped bar. Grab the bar with an even overhand grip (palms facing down). Bring your elbows to your sides, take a step back, and retract your shoulder blades. Engage your abs and take a breath. Extend your arms fully and hold the bottom position for a moment as you breathe out. Slowly bend your arms until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows, breathing in along the way.

title: Triceps Pushdown
howTo: Pick the appropriate load, set the pulley in the highest position, and attach a bar. Grab the bar with an even overhand grip and bring your elbows to your sides. Take a couple of steps back and lean your torso slightly forward. Engage your abs, retract your shoulder blades, and take a breath. Extend your arms while keeping your elbows to your sides and hold for a moment. Exhale. Slowly bend your arms until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows. Breathe in as your arms bend.

title: Triceps Rope Pushdown
howTo: Pick the appropriate weight, set the pulley to the highest position, and attach a rope. Grab the rope with both hands and bring your elbows to your sides. Take a step back and lean your torso forward slightly. Take a breath and extend your arms while keeping your elbows in position. Breathe out. Bend your arms slowly and breathe in. Stop when your wrists end up slightly higher than your elbows.

title: Upright Row (Barbell)
howTo: Grab a barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing back). Your hands should be shoulder-width apart. Stand tall, retract your shoulder blades, flex your abs, and take a breath. Row the barbell in a straight line from your hips to your chest and breathe out. Squeeze your upper back and biceps at the top. Extend your arms slowly and breathe in on the way down.

title: Upright Row (Cable)
howTo: Set the pulley to the lowest position and attach a straight bar. Select the appropriate weight. Lean forward and grab the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down). Stand up and step back to lift the weight off its stack. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and put one foot slightly forward for support. Take a breath and row the bar to your upper chest in one fluid motion. Exhale at the top. Slowly extend your arms as you breathe in.

title: Upright Row (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Have your feet in a comfortable stance, shoulders retracted, and arms straight. Position the dumbbells in front of your body and engage your abs. Inhale and pull the dumbbells in a straight vertical line toward your upper chest. Exhale at the top. Extend your arms, breathing in on the way down.

title: V Up
howTo: Lie on the floor with your legs straight, feet together, and arms stretched behind your head. Engage your abs and take a breath. Simultaneously raise your legs and upper body in one fluid motion, folding your body into a V shape and breathing out. Hold the top position for a moment and slowly lower your torso and legs as you breathe in. Don’t rest your feet on the floor between repetitions.

title: Vertical Traction (Machine)
howTo: Select the appropriate load. Face away from the machine with your backside close to the back pad with the handles overhead. Grab the handles with a neutral (palms facing one another) or overhand (palms facing forward) grip and sit down. Bring your chest out, engage your abs, and breathe in. Pull the handles down and to your sides with your elbows traveling in a semi-arc toward your lats. Exhale. Slowly extend your arms and breathe in.

title: Waiter Curl (Dumbbell)
howTo: Hold a dumbbell vertically and place your hands flat against the underside of the top weight plate with your thumbs on top for grip support. Stand tall, spread your feet slightly wider than your hips, and lean forward slightly. Bring your chest out, engage your abs, and breathe in. Curl the dumbbell until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows and pause for a moment as you exhale. Slowly lower the weight until your arms are fully extended. Breathe in on the way down. Repeat.

title: Walking Lunge
howTo: Retract your shoulder blades and engage your abs. Take a breath and extend your right leg forward, planting the foot on the floor. Immediately sink into a lunge until your back knee is almost in contact with the ground. Press through your front foot to extend the leg as you exhale. Breathe in as you lift your back foot off the floor and extend the back leg forward, planting the foot in front of your body and descending into another lunge. Continue moving forward until you’re done.

title: Walking Lunge (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and stand tall. Retract your shoulder blades and engage your abs. Take a breath and extend your right leg forward, planting the foot on the floor. Immediately sink into a lunge until your back knee is almost in contact with the ground. Press through your front foot to extend the leg as you exhale. Breathe in as you lift your back foot off the floor and extend the back leg forward, planting the foot in front of your body and descending into another lunge. Continue moving forward until you’re done.

title: Wall Ball
howTo: Grab a medium-sized medicine ball and stand in front of a wall. Lift the ball in front of your chest and keep your elbows bent. Have your feet in a comfortable stance for squats, with your toes pointing slightly out. Engage your abs and take a breath. Descend into a squat, keeping your heels in contact with the floor. Move down until your thighs are parallel to the floor, and press through your heels to move up. As your legs straighten, throw the ball up and against the wall and exhale. Immediately grab the ball, take another breath, and descend into another squat.

title: Wall Sit
howTo: Face away from a wall and stand a few inches away. Carefully lean back and position your upper back and buttocks against the wall. Engage your abs and slowly bend your knees to descend into a squat. Adjust your foot position and have your ankles underneath your knees. Your thighs should be roughly parallel to the floor. Bring your arms in front of your body or have them to your sides. Hold the position for 30 to 60 seconds, breathing steadily as you do.

title: Wide Pull Up
howTo: Reach up and grab a pull-up bar with a wide overhand grip (palms facing forward). Your hands should be more than shoulder-width apart but not too wide. Bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and lift your feet off the floor to suspend yourself in the air. Keep your feet together or bend your knees and interlock your lower legs. Take a breath and pull yourself up in one fluid motion. Go up until your chin is over the bar and hold for a moment as you exhale. Slowly lower yourself and breathe in.

title: Wide-Elbow Triceps Press (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells and sit on a flat gym bench. Place the weights on top of your thighs and hold them steady. Lie back with your elbows bent and the weights close to your body. Press the dumbbells to extend your arms. Your palms should face forward (toward your lower body). Plant your feet on the floor, bring your shoulders back, engage your abs, and breathe in. Slowly bend your elbows to bring the dumbbells inward and toward your chest. This will force your elbows to flare out to some degree. Carefully lower the weights on your chest and extend your arms as you exhale.

title: Wrist Roller
howTo: Grab a wrist roller and attach a weight plate to it. Stand tall and lift your arms in front of your body with your palms facing down. Bring your shoulders back and engage your abs. Inhale and begin rolling the plate up. Once the plate reaches your hands, roll in the opposite direction to lower the weight to the floor. Breathe steadily and roll for as long as possible.

title: Zercher Squat
howTo: Position the barbell at roughly belly button height. Walk up to the bar and press the crease of your elbows against it. Bend your arms to create a shelf and press the barbell against your stomach. Take a couple of steps back, bring your chest out, and take a deep breath into your belly. Squat by imagining that you’re sitting down in an invisible chair. Keep your heels on the floor and maintain an upright torso. Go down as comfortably as you can and push through your heels to move to the top position. Exhale.

title: Zottman Curl (Dumbbell)
howTo: Grab a pair of dumbbells, stand tall, and position your arms to your sides. Have the weights in front of your upper thighs with your palms facing forward. Engage your abs and take a breath. Curl the dumbbells up until your wrists are slightly higher than your elbows, and breathe out. As you reach the top position, rotate your wrists 180 degrees and lower the dumbbells to the starting position with your palms facing down. Breathe in on the way down. Rotate your wrists again and repeat.
